description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Berlin Bilingual School (BBS) provides affordable bilingual English-German education for native-level speaking students from Grade 1 to 13, emphasizing a top-notch curriculum from foundational learning to the bilingual Abitur.[1][2][4] The school celebrates diversity across 30+ nationalities, fostering creative and critical thinking to develop responsible global citizens, with a family-like atmosphere highlighted by students and staff.[1][2][4] Core values include embracing diversity of thought, experience, and culture; inclusion; anti-racism; sex positivity; and personal development through topics like empathy, nonviolent communication, drug education, online safety, and anti-bullying.[1][4][5]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 2007 by expat parents as an extension of a bilingual kindergarten (Berlin Kids International, started earlier in eastern Berlin), BBS opened its primary school that year in a renovated former East German building; it expanded to secondary in 2012, renamed itself, and split campuses in 2014.[1][2][3][4] Governed by Berlin Kids International e.V. and Pfefferwerk Stadtkultur gGmbH, it serves ~450 students from 30 nationalities, with ~100 staff from 25 nationalities; class sizes are 24-26 (primary) and 12-24 (secondary).[2] Primary campus: Weinstraße 1, 10249 Berlin (Friedrichshain); secondary (ISS/Gymnasium) campus: Granitzstr. 38A, 13189 Berlin (Pankow district).[1][2][3] No specific facilities details beyond campuses and school hours (primary: 8:30am-4pm, supervision 7:30-8:30am, afternoon to 6pm; secondary: 8am-4pm).[2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
BBS follows a bilingual English-German program: primary (Years 1-6) uses Berlin State Curriculum and International Primary Curriculum (IPC)/UK National Curriculum; secondary (Years 7-13, as Integrierte Sekundarschule) blends Berlin State Curriculum (Berliner Rahmenlehrplan), UK National Curriculum, with sciences in English, humanities in German, languages (incl. French/Spanish) by native speakers, and electives (IT, Drama, Psychology, Philosophy) from Grade 9.[1][2][5] Graduation includes Mittlere Schulabschluss (MSA) pathways, International/Edexcel IGCSE (Year 11, English/Science), and bilingual Abitur (Years 12/13, highest German qualification for university access).[1][2][5] Personal Development Curriculum addresses modern challenges like relationships and anti-racism.[5]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Small classes promote individualized learning, self-reflection, and community immersion, with project-based activities like Grade 9 acid-alkali experiments on indigestion tablets.[1][5] School life includes internships (e.g., Grade 9 in Jan 2026), Personal Development projects on sex education, anti-racism, and online safety, plus a family-like environment where students report feeling happy and excited.[1][4][5] Events feature open houses, conferences, and after-school programs ("Hort"); no specific sports, arts, or exchanges detailed, though drama elective and blog posts suggest hands-on science and presentations.[1][2][5]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Secondary students achieve excellent academic results and view BBS as a second home; the school prepares for bilingual Abitur with university access in Germany and internationally.[2][5] No awards, rankings, or specific outcome metrics (e.g., Abitur pass rates) mentioned.[1][2][4][5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
No enrollment process or requirements detailed in content (e.g., native-level German/English proficiency implied).[1][2][4] No fee information found on scraped pages; no PDFs listed with fees. Open houses (e.g., secondary: 21.01.2026, 16:00-19:00) and webinars suggest inquiry-based admissions.[1]

description:
# Berlin British School - Comprehensive School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

Berlin British School is committed to providing **holistic education** that balances academic excellence with personal development[1]. The school's core mission emphasizes nurturing character development through an engaging curriculum and supportive pastoral care, with a focus on preparing students as **life-long enthusiastic learners and global thinkers**[1].

The school combines **the best of British, international and German education** to create an inclusive learning environment[1]. Key educational principles include:

- High academic standards with small class sizes supporting personalized learning[1]
- Strong pastoral care focused on fostering student self-confidence[1]
- Holistic development addressing academic, social, and emotional growth[1]
- Inquiry-based learning processes aligned with international best practices[1]
- Recognition that education extends beyond academics to include enrichment activities and work-life balance[2]

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and Structure:**
Berlin British School operates across three sites near Berlin's historic Olympic stadium and Grunewald forest[1]. The secondary school is located in the leafy surroundings of Grunewald in the Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf district[2].

**Community:**
The school serves approximately 500 students aged 3-18 from diverse cultural backgrounds[5]. It welcomes both local Berlin families and international families, including children of embassy staff[8]. The school emphasizes creating a **warm and caring community** where students develop meaningful friendships and connections with teachers[1].

**Facilities:**
The school provides appropriate facilities for each year group and maintains an all-day school structure with extracurricular activities and after-school care[1].

**Governance:**
Berlin British School gGmbH operates as a not-for-profit limited company. All shares are owned by Berlin British School e.V., a registered association under German law whose purpose is promoting British education and culture in Germany[1].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Age Range and Structure:**
The school serves students aged 3-18, organized into Early Years, Primary School, and Secondary School[1].

**Languages of Instruction:**
The school offers **two educational streams**[2]:

- **Monolingual stream**: Taught entirely in English[2]
- **Bilingual stream**: Teaching in both English and German, available for students with German nationality and those planning long-term residence in Berlin[4]

Bilingual stream students must be fluent in written and spoken German[4]. German subjects in the bilingual stream include History, Ethics, Political Education, Economics-Work-Technology (WAT), and PE, while Mathematics is taught in both languages[4].

**Secondary School Qualifications:**

**Grades 9-10 (Key Stage 4):**
- All students work toward the **International General Certificate of Secondary Education (IGCSE)**[6]
- Bilingual stream students also take the **German MSA (Mittlerer Schulabschluss)**, the German intermediate school-leaving qualification[6]
- Students sit both MSA and IGCSE exams in spring of Grade 10[6]

**Grades 11-12:**
All students pursue the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**, described as the "gold standard" of international education[1]. The IB DP is offered in English only[4].

**Progression Requirements:**

*Monolingual students* require six IGCSE grades at Grade C or above, including Mathematics and English[6].

*Bilingual students* require four IGCSEs at Grade C or above (including English and Maths) and successful completion of all MSA examinations with an overall score under 3.0[6].

**IB Diploma Curriculum:**
Students choose six academic subjects (three at Standard Level, three at Higher Level) across six groups[1]:

- Group 1: Studies in Language and Literature (English A, German A, Self-Taught A)
- Group 2: Language Acquisition (German B, French B, Spanish B, German ab initio)
- Group 3: Individuals and Societies (Economics, Geography, History, Psychology)
- Group 4: Sciences (Biology, Chemistry, Physics, Environmental Systems and Societies)
- Group 5: Mathematics (Approaches and Analysis, Applications and Interpretations)
- Group 6: The Arts and Electives (Music, Visual Arts, Theatre Studies)

**Additional IB Requirements:**
- **Theory of Knowledge (ToK)**: Introduces philosophical ideas and critical thinking about knowledge[1]
- **Creativity, Action and Service (CAS)**: Minimum 150 hours of community service, creative expression, and personal development[1]
- **Extended Essay**: 4,000-word independent research project[1]

**Important Admissions Notes:**
The school does not admit students to Grade 10 or Grade 12[1]. Starting in Grades 9 and 11 is unlikely due to the two-year design of IGCSE and IB Diploma courses[1].

**Certification:**
Berlin British School is an **IB World School** running the Primary Years Programme (PYP), IGCSE, and IB Diploma programmes[6]. The school holds official status as a **'staatlich anerkannte bilinguale Grundschule'** (state-recognized bilingual primary school), **'genehmigte bilinguale Integrierte Sekundarschule'** (approved bilingual integrated secondary school), and **'Ergänzungsschule'** (supplementary school)[1].

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Extracurricular Activities:**
Secondary school students are encouraged to participate in a wide range of activities including[2]:

- GISST sports (competitive sports program)
- Model United Nations
- Various other clubs and activities enabling students to explore passions and develop leadership skills

**School Day Structure:**
- Lessons begin at 8:30 and end at 15:20 (3:20 PM)[2]
- School day officially ends at 16:00 (4:00 PM)[2]
- **After School Clubs** available until 16:30 (4:30 PM) or 17:00 (5:00 PM) for bilingual students[2][4]
- Before-school supervision available from 8:00 AM[2]

**Pastoral Care and Well-being:**
The school demonstrates deep commitment to student well-being through supportive teacher relationships, approachable administration, and solution-oriented problem-solving[1]. The school emphasizes maintaining work-life balance and addresses the health and well-being of all students[1].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

**University Placement:**
Following education at Berlin British School, students have been accepted into top universities in Germany, the UK, the USA, and worldwide[1].

**IB Diploma Results:**
The school publishes previous IB Diploma results, though specific data is referenced but not detailed in the provided content[1].

**International Recognition:**
As an IB World School, the school operates internationally acclaimed programmes recognized globally[1]. The IGCSE qualification is one of the world's most recognized qualifications and serves as a foundation for higher-level courses[7].

## 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES

**Admissions Process:**

1. **Enquiry Form**: Initial contact to receive information[1]
2. **Application Submission**: Detailed application form with school reports from the last two years; no cost or commitment to apply[1]
3. **School Tour**: Individual tours (30-45 minutes) of appropriate campus once a place is available[1]
4. **Student Visit**: Free three-day visit (two mornings for Kindergarten) allowing students and teachers to get acquainted; school evaluates academic level and social adaptability[1]
5. **Admission Decision**: Teachers, Head of School, and School Principal make final decisions based on visit feedback[1]

**Timing:**
Admission is generally possible at any time during the academic year if places are available, except Grade 10 and Grade 12[1].

**Fees:**

The school website indicates that **annual school fees and additional fees are available** but does not display specific EUR amounts on the main pages[1]. Instead, the school provides separate fee documents for different school types:

- **Kita-Fees 2025-2026** (Early Years/Kita)
- **EGS-Fees 2025-2026** (International School/Ergänzungsschule)
- **ESS-Fees 2025-2026** (Ersatzschule - state-recognized bilingual primary and secondary school)
- **ESS-Schulgeldordnung 2025-2026** (Fee regulations)
- **Antrag Einkommensabhängiges Schulgeld 2025-2026** (Income-dependent tuition application)[1]

**Additional Fees (Potential):**
- After School Care[1]
- Lunch Service[1]
- Extra Curricular Activities[1]
- School & Sport Uniform[1]
- Music Lessons[1]

**Scholarships and Financial Support:**
- **Income-based tuition options** are available[1]
- Parents should contact the Admissions team regarding fee schedules, income-related fees, or scholarships[1]
- The school does **not accept Kita Gutschein** (Kita vouchers) for Early Years[1]

**Important Note:**
Exact tuition amounts in EUR are not displayed on the publicly accessible website pages. Parents must contact the school directly or access the PDF fee documents (ESS-Fees 2025-2026, EGS-Fees 2025-2026, etc.) to obtain specific pricing information.

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Berlin Metropolitan School (BMS) strives for **academic excellence and strength in character** to contribute to a more informed world and an open-minded society.[1][5][6] The school's philosophy emphasizes international mindedness, global engagement, and respect for diverse cultures, fostering curiosity, empathy, and reflective thinking through inquiry-based learning inspired by IB principles.[web:scraped Curriculum Page] Teaching and learning promote engaged, self-directed learners who take positive action, with character building embedded via the IB Learner Profile, resilience, and self-awareness.[web:scraped Curriculum Page] BMS commits to adapting to societal changes, preparing students for future challenges with critical thinking, personal responsibility, and a balanced whole-day program from 7:30 a.m. to 6:00 p.m.[web:scraped About Page][1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in **2004**, BMS is the oldest and largest international school in Berlin-Mitte, initially bilingual and focused on gifted students, transitioning to an integrated secondary school with an IB profile in 2006.[1][2][4][7] It serves over **1,000-1,135 students** from **60-65 nationalities** and **22 home languages**, with a diverse community of German nationals and international families in a dynamic urban setting surrounded by museums and galleries.[web:scraped Main Page][web:scraped About Page][1][3][4][5][6] Facilities include **2 libraries**, **2 science labs**, digital infrastructure (Apple MacBooks, iPads, iMacs, Office 365, classroom displays), an auditorium, cafeteria, rooftop extensions, music rooms, roof garden, and sports areas in prefabricated GDR-era buildings expanded for senior classes.[web:scraped Main Page][3][7] Support services encompass career/university counseling, student support, learning/language assistance, school counselors, nurses, and child protection officers.[web:scraped Main Page]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
BMS offers a **K-12 continuum** (ages 3-18) as an all-day **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** in Berlin-Mitte, combining international and German national curricula in **English** (primary) and **German** (alternate).[web:scraped School Information][1][3][4] Programs include:
- **Early Learning Center** (ages 3-6): IB Primary Years Programme (PYP) and Berlin State Curriculum.
- **Primary School** (Grades 1-6, ages 6-11): Foundation for education, IB PYP elements.
- **Secondary School** (Grades 7-8, ages 11-14): Bespoke BMS and Berlin State Curriculum.
- **Grades 9-10** (ages 14-16): IGCSE, Berlin State Curriculum, Berliner Bildungsgriffe.
- **Grades 11-12**: International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP), bilingual options, German National School Diploma (Mittlerer Schulabschluss - MSA).[web:scraped Main Page][3][6]
Graduation pathways: **IGCSE**, **IBDP** (average score 34, above world average of 27), **MSA**; no Abitur or BBR mentioned.[web:scraped Main Page][3][4] Comprehensive language program supports school languages and additions like Mandarin, Spanish.[web:scraped About Page]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
BMS provides a **whole-day program** (7:30 a.m.-6:00 p.m.) with **After School Program** extending learning, plus **75 clubs** in arts (ballet, hip-hop, theater), languages (Mandarin, Spanish), sports (basketball, capoeira, parkour), and STEM (coding, Science Explorers, Arduino).[web:scraped Main Page] Sport teams compete nationally/internationally; over **70 students** in leadership roles; class excursions, camps, and hands-on field learning.[web:scraped Main Page] Events include talent shows, winter concerts (350+ performers, 1,000 visitors), MUN conferences (awards at Oxford MUN 2025), guided tours, and open houses.[web:scraped Main Page]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
**Average IB DP score: 34** (above world average); strong IGCSE/DP results and university destinations documented annually.[web:scraped Main Page][3] Students earned multiple awards at **2025 Oxford MUN** for research, debating, and global issues understanding.[web:scraped Main Page] Accreditations: **IB World School**, **Cambridge International School**, **CIS**, **NEASC**, Berlin Senate-authorized; memberships in AGIS, ECIS.[3][4][5][6] Consistent academic excellence K-12, with 59% pursuing bilingual IBDP.[web:scraped Main Page]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions involve online applications via Parent Portal/Apply, guided tours (e.g., Early Learning Jan 22, Secondary Jan 26, 2026), virtual open houses, and contracts (ongoing, auto-renew Aug 1-Jul 31; 3-month notice, reducible to 1 month for moves; hold option with retainer).[web:scraped Main Page][web:scraped Admissions Page][web:scraped Fees Page] Non-selective, welcoming diverse families/staff committed to community goals.[web:scraped Admissions Page][5][6] **Fees are income-based** (sliding scale on total positive income, calculated via PDF sheet, paid monthly by direct debit; sibling discounts; annual publication on website).[web:scraped Fees Page][4][6] Specifics:
- **Admission/Registration Fee**: One-time **€1,100**, due on contract signing (or Grade 1 entry for Early Learning).[4][web:scraped Fees Page]
- **Tuition**: Monthly **€210-€1,340** (grade/income-dependent); exact 2026 structure in PDF: https://www.metropolitanschool.com/fileadmin/user_upload/pdf/tuition/BMS_Tuition_2026_ENG.pdf; calculator: https://www.metropolitanschool.com/fileadmin/user_upload/pdf/tuition/bms_calculating_school_tuition_rate_01.pdf.[4][web:scraped Fees Page]
- **Analog Media Fee**: Annual (October), for books, materials, equipment (amount in PDFs).[web:scraped Fees Page]
- **Digital Media Fee**: Annual (February), for tech/infrastructure (amount in PDFs).[web:scraped Fees Page]
- **Meals**: Early Learning **€23/month** (breakfast, lunch, snack); Primary free; Secondary **€5.10/day** via Dussmann contract.[web:scraped Fees Page]
- **After School/Day Care**: Vouchers via Youth Welfare Office (Early Learning kindergarten voucher; Grades 1-6 Hort Gutschein 1:30-6:00 p.m.).[web:scraped Fees Page]
- **Other**: Class trips/exams invoiced separately (IB/Cambridge fees passed on).[web:scraped Fees Page]
- **Financial Aid/Scholarships**: Available via application (PDF: https://www.metropolitanschool.com/fileadmin/user_upload/pdf/tuition/bms_application_for_financial_aid.pdf); case-specific for income changes/emergencies.[web:scraped Fees Page]
Direct debit: https://www.metropolitanschool.com/fileadmin/user_upload/pdf/tuition/bms_sepa_direkt_debit_mandate_2017.pdf.[web:scraped Fees Page]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
BEST-Sabel-Oberschule emphasizes individualized potential development, blending Gymnasium and Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) pathways to Abitur tailored to students' learning pace, with profiles in **Wirtschaft & Berufsorientierung** and **Sprachen**.[1][2][4] The philosophy prioritizes a familial atmosphere in small classes (max 22 students), holistic education beyond knowledge transmission, and integration of life and learning in a campus-like setting with bound full-day operations (8-16 Uhr) including self-organized learning, AGs (working groups), and individual support like TiF for reading/writing difficulties.[1][2][3] As part of BEST-Sabel Bildungszentrum, active since 1991, it fosters reliable education from Kita to vocational completion, focusing on social competencies, practical orientation, and respectful interactions.[Main Page][1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded within BEST-Sabel Bildungszentrum (active since 1991), the Oberschule occupies a historic, listed building at Lindenstr. 1, 12555 Berlin (Köpenick, Treptow-Köpenick district), originally built in 1910 as a boys' school by Hugo Kinzer and hosting BEST-Sabel programs since 2008.[2][3] A modern Oberstufenhaus extension supports upper grades since 2024/2025, creating a spacious campus with familial atmosphere.[1][5] Facilities include library, computer rooms, whiteboards, teaching kitchen, wood workshop, and bound full-day setup with meals; community ties involve local partners like Reiterhof "Pferde Land," BIZ, and initiatives like sustainable coffee cup projects with Bürgerstiftung Treptow-Köpenick.[3][Fees/Tuition Page][1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Serves grades 7+ as state-recognized private Ersatzschule (ISS-Gymnasium), offering flexible switches between **Gymnasium** (Abitur after 12 years) and **ISS** (Abitur after 13 years) until grade 11, plus **Fachoberschule (FOS)** for Fachabitur (Wirtschaft or Sozialpädagogik) in 2 years post-grade 10.[1][2][4] Profiles: Wirtschaft/Berufsorientierung (with praxis weeks, internships, company ties) and Sprachen; more English than Berlin standard, S1 Englisch/Französisch, S2 Englisch/Latein, S4 Englisch/Spanisch; math/science focus, theater, PC/laptop training all grades.[3][2][4] Bound Ganztag includes differentiated instruction, Lerngruppen for reading/math, and pathways to MSA/Fachabitur.[1][3]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Full-day rhythm (8-16 Uhr) features self-organized learning, AGs, sports (rudern grade 9, Skischule grade 8, swimming/segeln due to water proximity), robotics (VEX IQ), Känguru math contests, theater/musicals, project weeks (Demokratie, sustainability, fitness trips), excursions (Bio-Apfelhof, museums), and social initiatives (anti-racism, donations).[Main Page][3][5][8] Extracurriculars build talents via Freizeitaktivitäten, sports groups, and practicals (Betriebspraktikum, social Praktikum).[3][1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or certifications extracted; highlights include world record (unspecified, Grundschule-linked), VEX Robotics participation, sustainable projects (biodegradable coffee cup with Bürgerstiftung), Erasmus+ to Iceland, and 20-year Oberschule milestone with new Oberstufenhaus.[Main Page][5][Fees/Tuition Page] Prüfungsergebnisse available via Berlin.de portrait.[3]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions support quereinstieg any grade, open doors (e.g., 30 Sep 2025, 11 Oct 2025), info events; alternatives for no Gymnasium placement, with three Abitur paths.[4][5][6] No exact fee amounts mentioned in content; no PDFs with fees found. Contact: +49 30 280360970, oberschule@best-sabel.de.[3]

Lunch available through catering partner (additional cost). Scholarships and special arrangements available.

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Canisius-Kolleg is a Jesuit school with a **christlich-humanistische** (Christian-humanistic) profile, emphasizing excellent education in a comprehensive, error-friendly sense to foster responsible personal development.[1] It promotes talent development, independent learning, critical and creative thinking through an open all-day program, while keeping the question of God alive, encouraging reflection, mindfulness, justice, solidarity, and respect for human dignity.[1] Jesuit values include nurturing judgment, freedom, and commitment to a just society, with digital leadership education from grade 9 using school tablets.[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded as a Gymnasium since **1925**, with the Integrierte Sekundarschule “Pedro Arrupe” added since **2019**.[1] It serves **929 students** (Gymnasium: 829; ISS: 100) from **60 nationalities** and **41 mother tongues**, fostering diversity as a hallmark of good education.[1] Located at **Tiergartenstr. 30-31, 10785 Berlin-Mitte**, near Tiergarten (0.01 km), Potsdamer Platz (1.5 km), Brandenburg Gate (2 km), and Breitscheidplatz (1.6 km).[1] Facilities support an open all-day program, with ~105-120 in afternoon care and ~690-710 in the on-site youth organization ISG; classes average ~30 students, smaller in upper secondary groups and electives.[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Offers **Gymnasium from grade 5 (Sexta)** leading to a 4-semester gymnasiale Oberstufe (upper secondary with profile "extras"), and **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) “Pedro Arrupe” from grade 9**, state-recognized including Sekundarstufe II.[1] Latin is compulsory from grade 5; open to all from grade 5, with lateral entries up to grade 10 (if places available) and into Oberstufe.[1] Catholic religious education for all (including non-Catholics; ~20% Protestant students receive their own); digital-supported teaching with tablets from grade 9; **handyfreie Schule** (phone-free).[1] ISS focuses on flight and migration; supports gifted education via "Zentrum für individuelle Begabungsförderung".[1] Graduation pathways include Abitur via Gymnasium Oberstufe; state-recognized ISS pathways (MSA implied, details in state recognition).[1][3]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
**Open Ganztagsangebot** (all-day program) with afternoon care (~105-120 participants), ISG youth organization (~690-710 members) for social/democratic skills.[1] Extracurriculars include AG-Programm with musical focus, school pastoral care, counseling, prevention, class/student councils.[1] International networking with Jesuit schools in USA, Lithuania, France, Near East; promotes peace, reconciliation, anti-antisemitism.[1] Kollegsgottesdienste (school services) weekly; diverse events like Ramadan observance.[1] Sports/arts not detailed beyond music AG and general talent development.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
**State recognition** for ISS Pedro Arrupe in Sekundarstufe II (recently achieved).[1] "Digital-Leadership-Education" implemented; Zentrum für individuelle Begabungsförderung established.[1] No specific awards, rankings, or outcome stats (e.g., Abitur rates) mentioned in content.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Admissions**: Online application for grade 5 (Sexta) 2026/2027 open 9 Sep 2025–19 Dec 2025 (12:30); process includes Aufnahmeverfahren; lateral entries to grade 10/Oberstufe if places available; contact Frau Kaminska for queries.[1] Open to all, emphasizing diversity.[1]

**Fees**: Tuition is **income-scaled** (Schulgeldstaffelung) with moderate base rate; no exact EUR amounts listed on pages—detailed in PDF: https://canisius.de/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Canisius-Kolleg_Schulgeldinormationen_2025-2027.pdf.[1] School commits "am Schulgeld wird der Besuch ... nicht scheitern" (tuition will not prevent attendance); reductions/full waivers via simple written self-assessment request to Rektor (no financial disclosure required).[1] Additional SEPA mandates for Nachmittagsbetreuung (after-school), Lernmittel (materials).[1] Jesuit order, alumni, donors subsidize; no church tax funding.[1] No registration, meal, or specific material/after-school costs listed (after-school via separate SEPA).[1] Scholarships via donor network; income-based options explicitly available.[1]

tuition_notes: Canisius-Kolleg uses an income-based tuition model (Schulgeldstaffelung) for school years 2025-2027. The base tuition (Basissatz) is EUR 198.00 per month for families with annual income up to EUR 76,000. An entry-level tuition (Einstiegsschulgeld) of EUR 100.00 per month is available for families with annual income up to EUR 29,420 (gross). The maximum tuition cap (Höchstsatz) is EUR 396.00 per month. From grade 9 onwards, an additional fee for school materials (Tablets) applies. A one-time administrative fee (Verwaltungsgebühr) equal to one base tuition rate (EUR 198.00) is required upon contract signature and credited toward the first monthly payment. Tuition reduction or complete waiver is available upon request based on family self-assessment, with no formal income documentation required. Sibling discounts apply: 75% of base rate for second child, 50% for third child, and no tuition for fourth and subsequent children. The school operates on the principle that school attendance does not fail due to tuition fees (Bildung scheitert nicht am Schulgeld). For families receiving Berlinpass (unemployment/welfare benefits), tuition can be reduced to EUR 0.00-99.00 or fully waived.

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Evangelische Schule Charlottenburg operates under the motto **"verstehen bilden handeln"** (understand, educate, act), emphasizing strengthening children and youth to unfold their potential, assume responsibility for themselves, others, and creation, and provide a solid foundation for further life and education.[1][2][3] As an evangelical school in the sponsorship of the Evangelische Schulstiftung EKBO, it is open to diverse faiths and worldviews, with evangelical religious education as a core subject focusing on questions of belief, ethics, and responsible living in a pluralistic world.[1] The philosophy prioritizes individual support, potential development, and ethical responsibility, guided by Christian values like "Gott spricht: Ich mache alles neu!" (God says: I make all things new!).[1][2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
No specific founding date is mentioned in the content. The school is located at **Guerickestr. 4-6, 10587 Berlin (Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf district)**, serving as a private evangelical institution under the Evangelische Schulstiftung EKBO.[1][5][8][9] It fosters a close-knit community with small class sizes (two-stream ISS to prevent anonymity), dual tutors per class, sonderpädagogik support across all grades, a school social worker, and partnerships with external providers like Intellego for after-school tutoring.[1][4][6] Facilities include an open all-day program (Hort/Ganztagsschule) with dedicated spaces (e.g., Ganztagsbereich at Alt Lietzow 30), a school canteen (Schulmensa), and digital learning via the "it's learning" platform since 2019 for materials, individualized paths, grades, and homework.[1] Staff includes expanded leadership, subject coordinators, teachers, social pedagogues, a janitor, secretaries, and trainees.[5][6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school spans **grades 1-10** as a combined primary and **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)**, leading to **mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA), MSA+, or (e)BBR** (no Abitur; not a Gemeinschaftsschule, requiring new applications post-grade 10).[1][2][4] Core elements include evangelical religious education as a main subject, mandatory elective courses (Wahlpflichtunterricht): grade 5 offers artistic/musical/handcrafting/culinary options; grade 6 focuses on social learning; grades 7-10 require binding choices in "Arbeits- und Ernährungslehre" (work/nutrition, including food processing, wood/textile work, computers), **French**, or **natural sciences**.[curriculum page] Languages include German, French (elective), and subject-specific instruction; career preparation integrates across programs with berufsorientierung and business/work/technic courses.[1][curriculum page] Support includes diagnostics, individual Förderung in all sonderpädagogik areas, and collaborations with upper secondary centers and evangelical schools.[1][4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in **open all-day care (Hort/Ganztagsschule)** with tutoring hours, working groups (Arbeitsgemeinschaften), elective courses in arts, music, crafts, social learning, French, sciences, and practical skills like computer and material processing.[1][curriculum page] Additional offerings include career counseling (Berufsorientierung), student advising, Freiwilligendienste (voluntary services), school partnerships, and a gallery of activities; sports, art, and music are coordinated across primary and secondary levels.[1][4][6][curriculum page] Digital tools via "it's learning" enable personalized learning; no specific exchanges or sports details are listed, but practical berufsleben preparation occurs throughout.[1][curriculum page]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No awards, rankings, or specific certifications are mentioned. The school aims for all students to achieve an ability-appropriate **(e)BBR, MSA, or MSA+** without leaving without a qualification, supported by tutoring, partnerships, and post-grade 10 pathways to upper schools or apprenticeships.[4]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Enrollment process**: Download and submit the Anmeldeformular (intake application) in person at the secretariat, by post, or password-protected email (due to data protection); separate form for OGS (open Ganztagsschule).[admissions page] Contact secretariat (Nicole Jahnke, Kathleen Mrowietz; 030 343571610/620; hours: Mo 08:00-13:30, Di/Do/Fr 08:00-14:30, Mi 08:00-10:00/13:00-14:30) or Ganztagsbereich (Monika Lemke; 030 33091894; Mo 14-16 or by appointment).[5][admissions page] No requirements specified beyond forms; personal discussions or open days available.[1]

**Fees**: No tuition amounts, registration fees, material fees, meal costs, after-school care costs, scholarships, or income-based options are mentioned on web pages. PDFs (e.g., Grund- und Hausordnung) are listed but marked as unlikely fee-related; contact school for details.

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Evangelische Schule Neukölln (ESN) follows a **holistic pedagogy** that promotes independent thinking, feeling, and acting among students through work, play, and celebration.[1] It operates as a state-recognized public school rooted in the Christian tradition of the Bible and history, maintained by the Schulstiftung der Evangelischen Kirche Berlin-Brandenburg-schlesische Oberlausitz, while embracing ecumenical and interreligious dialogue and welcoming students from other confessions and religions.[1] The Christian profile is evident in mandatory religion classes, devotions, and services, with opportunities to address ethical issues from a Christian perspective across subjects; it contributes to educational equity by avoiding early tracking into specific school tracks.[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
No specific founding date or historical details are provided in the content.[1] The school is located at **Mainzer Str. 47, 12053 Berlin** in the Neukölln district and serves as a private, state-recognized institution open to diverse religious backgrounds.[1] Contact details include phone (+49(0)30 624 20 27), fax (+49(0)30 624 20 28), and email (buero@evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de), with secretariat hours: Mon/Wed/Fri 08:00–13:45, Tue/Thu 08:00–15:00.[1] No explicit facilities details are mentioned beyond a coordinating educator in the ground-floor Hort (after-school care) and standard classroom structures implied by lesson times.[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
ESN spans **classes 1-13**, combining a Grundschule (primary), Integrierte Sekundarschule (integrated secondary), and gymnasiale Oberstufe (upper secondary), allowing all general education qualifications without early tracking.[1] Subjects include **Biologie, Kunst, Chemie, Deutsch, Englisch, Geografie, Geschichte, Mathematik, Musik, Physik, Religion, Sport, and WAT (grades 8/9)**.[1] Religion is mandatory, integrated with Christian ethics across subjects; the school is state-recognized, enabling standard Berlin qualifications like MSA (Mittlerer Schulabschluss), Fachabitur, and Abitur.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars emphasize music and history: Class 10A's "Musik macht Geschichte" project in Oranienburg (2025/26), Class 10c's remembrance trip to Krakau and Auschwitz (Nov 2025), and music trips by Sek I choir and orchestra AG.[1] Community activities include a devotion on the Ukraine war by teachers and students, student council meetings (SV-Tagung 21.11.2025), and Hort programs during autumn holidays 2025.[1] Sports and arts are core subjects; no exchanges are specified.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No awards, rankings, certifications, or specific outcomes (e.g., graduation rates) are mentioned in the content.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Enrollment deadlines**: Grade 1: Fri, 19.06.2026; Grade 7: Wed, 21.01.2026 (today is 20.01.2026, so Grade 7 deadline is tomorrow).[1] Detailed info in PDFs: Grade 7 process (https://evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Informationen-zum-Anmeldeverfahren-fuer-die-7.-Klasse-2026_2027.pdf) and Grade 1 (https://evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Anmeldung-ESN-1.-Klasse-zum-01.08.2027-AKTUELL.pdf).[1] After-school care (Hort) for grades 1-6: Free core from 7:30-13:30 (halbtags Grundschule); paid modules: 6:00-7:30 (early), 13:30-16:00 (afternoon, free for grades 1-2), 16:00-18:00 (late); combinable, with ferienbetreuung for grades 5-6 (included for 1-4).[1] Apply via Betreuungsgutschein at Amt für Tagesbetreuung, then school contract; forms: Antrag auf Betreuung (https://evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/antrag-efoeb-ogb.pdf), Hinweise (https://evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de/wp-content/uploads/2018/12/Unterlagen-Hort.pdf), Erläuterungen zur Beteiligung (https://evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/erlaeuterungen-zur-kostenfestsetzung-2018-hort.pdf), Erklärung (https://evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/erklaerung-fuer-die-festsetzung-der-hortkosten.pdf).[1] **Tuition**: Income-dependent Schulgeld (details on Schulstiftung homepage, not specified here); no exact EUR amounts for tuition, registration, materials, or meals listed on pages.[1] Contact coordinator Frau Stöwe (Tel: 6200991-32, stoewe@evangelische-schule-neukoelln.de) for care queries.[1]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's mission emphasizes "Lernen mit Freunden" (Learning with Friends), fostering a familial atmosphere where social learning, successful collaboration, and Christian values like "Voneinander lernen. Miteinander arbeiten. Fürinander leben." (Learn from each other. Work together. Live for each other) are central.[2][1] It promotes holistic education with a strong **sozial-diakonisches Profil** (social-diakonic profile), openness to all regardless of background, mandatory evangelical religious education, and participation in church events like Andachten, Gottesdienste, and school festivals.[2][1][9] The philosophy prioritizes stable frameworks for the entire school career, social behavior training, and relationships as key to successful learning.[1][5]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded around 1951 as a small one-room school in the Melanchthonhaus on the Evangelisches Johannesstift campus, the school celebrated its 75th anniversary in 2026, evolving into a comprehensive institution from Grundschule to Abitur.[5] It is owned by the Evangelische Schulstiftung Berlin-Brandenburg (EKBO Schulstiftung), a church foundation established in 2004 now overseeing 33 schools.[6][1] Located on the expansive Johannesstift grounds in Berlin-Spandau (Hakenfelde), it serves as a Stadtteilschule for northern Spandau and collaborates closely with Johannesstift facilities for elderly care, disability support, and youth services, including intergenerational projects like "Generationenbrücke."[1][3][2] Facilities include a main building with specialized rooms for music, chemistry, physics, cooking, crafts, computers, and an aula; pavilion-style classroom buildings; a Kunstraum; spaces in Gustav-Werner-Haus and Reinhard-Lange-Haus since 2008; and bright classrooms with flexible work areas.[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
This state-recognized private school offers continuous education from **Grundschule** (grades 1-6, zweizügig with year-homogeneous groups and cross-grade projects), **Integrierte Sekundarschule** (grades 7-10), to **gymnasiale Oberstufe** (grades 11-13, small courses).[1][2][3] Curriculum follows state Rahmenpläne and Versetzungsbeschlüsse, including evangelical religion as a core subject, technical-natural science in grade 4, differentiation in subjects like math, English, German, and chemistry, and dual learning elements like internships and social praktika.[1][8][9] Graduation pathways include **Berufsbildungsreife**, **Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA)**, and **Abitur**.[3] Inklusive programs like "Schule ohne Grenzen" integrate with the August Hermann Francke Schule; materials are loaned; assessments are process-oriented with verbal reports in early grades.[1][4][9]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
The school emphasizes social-diakonic engagement through collaborations with Johannesstift (e.g., social praktika, "Chance auf Schule" for grades 7-8 at-risk students), Wahlpflichtfach Sozial-Diakonisches Lernen, and projects like "Orientierungs- und Bildungszentrum" for school-to-work transitions.[3][5][8] Extracurriculars include jahrgangsübergreifende projects, Generationenbrücke visits to senior homes, außerschulische Bildungsangeboten focused on nature, sustainability, and inclusion, school events (Einschulungsfeste, Erntedankfest, Weihnachten, Fasching, Abschlussball), after-school Hort, and involvement in Wettbewerben.[1][3][2] Sports, arts, and exchanges are not explicitly detailed beyond Kunstraum, Musikraum, and Werkraum facilities.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or quantitative outcomes like Prüfungsergebnisse are detailed in the content, though the gymnasiale Oberstufe received Senatsverwaltung recognition around the 2025/2026 Jubiläum.[5] Achievements highlighted include growth from a small school to comprehensive coverage (Grundschule to Abitur), high student motivation in projects, and strong partnerships.[5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions require an **Anmeldebogen** (non-binding interest form) submitted to the Sekretariat, potentially followed by an Aufnahmegespräch or Hospitieren; no fixed criteria, but priority for siblings and internal transfers (e.g., Grundschule to class 7).[Admissions Page] Deadlines: Grundschule 17.10.2025; Sekundarschule class 7: 23.01.2026; Oberstufe via coordinator (*protected email*).[Admissions Page] Church membership desired but not required; openness to Christian content and mandatory religion/Events expected.[Admissions Page][9] Open spots in classes 7 and 11; high demand limits interviews.[Main Page] **Tuition**: Starts at **30€/month**, income- and family-size scaled (Ermäßigungen for siblings, full exemptions possible); details via Schulstiftung website.[Admissions Page][Curriculum Page] No specifics on registration, materials (loaned), meals (Mensaplan mentioned but no costs), or Hort fees. Scholarships via exemptions; income-based structure confirmed.

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Evangelische Schule Steglitz operates as a "Haus des Lebens und Lernens" (House of Life and Learning), emphasizing a **familial school atmosphere**, integration of **Evangelical religious education** as a regular subject, and alignment with state school frameworks for curricula and promotion decisions. Its school program, titled "Aus der Vergangenheit lernen – Gegenwart gestalten – Zukunft vorbereiten" (Learn from the past – shape the present – prepare for the future), serves as the "director's script" outlining key priorities for implementation, approved by the school conference on June 17, 2015.[1][2] Core values include community celebrations, festivals as part of school life, and a quote from Friedrich Fröbel: "Erziehung ist: Beispiel. Und Liebe. Sonst nichts." (Education is: example. And love. Nothing else.).[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in **1948**, this historic private school in southwest Berlin focuses on a supportive community environment, serving students from grades 1-13 in a church-sponsored (EKBO) structure.[1][2] Located at **Beymestr. 6-7, 12167 Berlin** in the **Steglitz-Zehlendorf district**, it fosters a diverse, inclusive community evident in events like the school festival on September 26, 2025, featuring shared activities for students, parents, teachers, alumni, and volunteers, including sports fields, gardens with home-grown vegetables, and sustainability exhibits.[1] Facilities support all-day care, animals on campus, and project days; no additional details on buildings or capacity provided.[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **state-recognized primary school (Grundschule), integrated secondary school (Integrierte Sekundarschule, ISS), and gymnasiale Oberstufe (upper secondary with gymnasium track)**, it covers **grades 1-13** with curricula and promotion rules matching state schools, including Evangelical religious instruction.[1] The integrated model supports pathways to **Abitur** via the gymnasiale Oberstufe; MSA (Mittlerer Schulabschluss) and BBR (Berufsbildungsreife) likely available through the ISS structure, though not explicitly stated.[1] Timetables (Zeittafel) and school program PDF detail scheduling; digital education emphasized via Fobizz certification.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in **diverse extracurriculars** including Arbeitsgemeinschaften (clubs), **sport courses**, music events, all-day care activities, and sustainability projects like vegetable gardens and peace-building crafts.[1] Community-building includes school festivals with games, markets, grilling, memory games, and swap meets; conflict mediation via Konfliktlotsen, student counseling, and class councils.[1] Ganztagsbetreuung (all-day care) features regular events; animals are present on campus; timetables support structured days.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school holds the **Fobizz-Siegel**, recognizing exceptional commitment to **digital education and teacher training**.[1] Successful community events like the 2025 school festival highlight creativity and diversity; no specific academic outcomes, rankings, or graduation rates mentioned.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Admissions** are level-specific: Grundschule (grade 1) via non-binding pre-registrations May to early summer holidays prior year, with lottery if oversubscribed; requires church affiliation form and feedback ~6 weeks post-summer break.[1] ISS (grade 7) deadline January 21, 2026, needing grade 5 half-year 2 report; feedback aligns with state timelines.[1] Gymnasiale Oberstufe (grade 11) from November to February 14 prior year, via secretariat.[1] Open days: January 13, 2026 (ISS/GO), January 16 (Sek II/GO).[1] Contact: Karen Meincke & Johanna Culjak, protected email, 030 7916001/0021.[1]

**Fees**: Monthly **Schulgeld starts at 30€**, income-scaled by family size and earnings; sibling discounts; full exemptions possible. Detailed on Schulstiftung website; no registration, material, meal, or after-school specifics listed (separate PDFs like Zeittafel may contain schedules but not confirmed fees).[1]

description:
# Freie Schule Berlin-Mahlsdorf School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

Freie Schule Berlin-Mahlsdorf is a **state-approved alternative school (Ersatzschule) based on anthroposophical principles**[3]. The school's pedagogical approach emphasizes holistic child development within an anthroposophical educational framework, though specific mission statements and core values are not detailed in the available website content.

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location and Structure:**
Freie Schule Berlin-Mahlsdorf is located in the Marzahn-Hellersdorf district at Elsenstraße 13A, 12623 Berlin[3][8]. The school operates as an integrated school (ISS) serving grades 1-11.

**Community:**
The school maintains an active parent community with structured parent involvement opportunities. The admissions process includes mandatory attendance at information evenings, suggesting emphasis on family alignment with the school's philosophy[user content].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

The school offers education from **grades 1 through 11** (Unterstufe, Mittelstufe, and Oberstufe)[3]. The curriculum is structured according to anthroposophical educational principles, though specific subject details and language offerings are not elaborated in the available content.

**Graduation Pathways:**
The school provides pathways to recognized German school certificates, though specific details about Abitur, MSA (Mittlerer Schulabschluss), or BBR (Berufsbildungsreife) offerings are not specified in the provided materials.

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

Limited information is available regarding extracurricular activities, sports programs, arts offerings, or student exchanges. The school maintains a structured calendar with published holiday schedules and events[3].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

No specific awards, certifications, or academic rankings are mentioned in the available website content.

## 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES

**Admissions Process:**

For **first-grade entry**, the process includes:
- Application submission by early-to-mid September (year before enrollment)
- Mandatory attendance at an information evening
- Family interviews in November to discuss family circumstances and school expectations
- Play afternoon in January where children interact without parents
- Final class composition and acceptance notification in February[user content]

The school receives more applications than available spaces for first grade and must decline some families[user content].

For **grades 2-11 (lateral entry)**, admission is generally possible at any time. The process requires submission of an application form, questionnaire, and copies of the last two school reports. Following a getting-to-know conversation and positive observation (Hospitation), a school contract can be concluded with a trial period allowing termination by either party[user content].

**Tuition and Fees:**

As a state-approved alternative school (Ersatzschule), Freie Schule Berlin-Mahlsdorf receives **93% of personnel costs from the Berlin state government** according to § 101 of the Berlin School Law. This subsidy includes contributions for material costs and school facility acquisition and operation[user content].

Due to the 7% funding gap, the school charges tuition to parents. **Specific tuition amounts are not listed on the main website pages**. The school maintains a detailed fee schedule (Schulgeldregelung) available as a PDF document[user content].

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Freie Schule Charlottenburg is a reform-pedagogical primary and secondary school emphasizing respect for children's developmental processes, inspired by Jesper Juul, Montessori, Piaget, and Wild. Its core philosophy centers on **self-determined learning**, freedom without compulsion, no grades or classwork, and holistic development fostering inner strength, self-respect, humanity, self-motivated action, creativity, social competence, and subject competence. The school prioritizes non-directive education, individuality, sensory-motor learning through movement, and ethical/philosophical exploration without affiliation to any religion or worldview.[1][6][9]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 2002 as a parent and educator initiative, the school became independently owned in August 2010 after transitioning from the Freie Schule am Mauerpark. It serves about 25 students with 4 core learning companions (Lernbegleiter) plus additional adults, volunteers, and parents in a community-oriented environment promoting democracy and self-determination. Facilities include 600 m² of open spaces (no closed classrooms), outdoor areas, and specialized rooms: Foyer (eating, games, reading, assemblies); Werkstatt & Kreativraum (crafting, building, experiments); Musikraum; plus areas for sciences/experiments, movement/sports/role-play, arts/crafts, math/Montessori materials/languages, reading/writing/math, music, and garden/courtyard. Children freely choose activities across fluid themes.[3][6][9]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school offers grades 1-10 (currently up to grade 7) as a state-approved Ersatzschule (integrated primary and secondary, approved to class 10), with open all-day programs, year-crossing groups in early years, and English as the first foreign language. Learning is child-led via prepared environments, offer plans, projects, and daily activities like movement, experimenting, crafting, building, role-play, theater, cooking, math, writing, reading, discussions, and life skills—no fixed schedules, grades, or tests. It emphasizes sensomotoric learning, self-accessible Montessori-oriented materials, and fächerübergreifend (cross-disciplinary) pursuits based on children's interests.[2][3][5][7][8][9]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in self-chosen, interest-driven activities across open spaces: movement/turnen/sports, exploring/experimenting, crafting/pottery/building, role-play/theater, cooking, math/writing/reading, discussions, music, games, arts/malerei/basteln, sciences, languages, and garden work. Extracurriculars integrate into the day with projects, team meetings, outings, and volunteer-supported pursuits; after-school care (Hort) extends to 15:00-16:00. No structured sports/arts/exchanges mentioned, but emphasis on holistic, body-mind-spirit involvement and social learning.[3][9]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No awards, rankings, certifications, or specific outcomes (e.g., Abitur/MSA/BBR rates) are mentioned in the content.[1-10]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Enrollment process**: Parents must engage deeply with the pedagogical concept and apply non-directive principles at home. Steps include: attending info events, submitting the Anmeldebogen (form), observation (Hospitation), and team parent interview. Contact Christine Pichel at 017663749499 or christine.pichel@freie-schule-charlottenburg.de for terms; primarily accepts first-graders, exceptions to grade 3, limited lateral entrants to grade 2 (secondary full). Early contact advised as spots fill quickly.[Admissions Page]

**Fees**:
- **Tuition**: Income-scaled **100-240 EUR monthly** (details in Schulgeldregelung); private pay.
- **After-school care (Hort)**: Senatsverwaltung-set module (Modul 2); amount not specified.
- **Development contribution**: Paid at enrollment, refundable only if leaving during probation; case-by-case social criteria.
- No specifics on registration/material/meal fees; further info at events. Anmeldebogen PDF may have details but not confirmed fee-related.[Admissions Page]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Freie Schule Pankow is a state-recognized alternative school emphasizing **freiwilligkeit (voluntariness)**, **selbstbestimmung (self-determination)**, and learning outside conventional systems, providing a safe space free from right-wing or extremist ideologies.[1][4][5] It follows the tradition of Freie Alternativschulen, prioritizing a humanistic view that grants all individuals dignity, self-determination, and lifelong education through partnerships between children and adults, fostering social relationships, identity, and creative conflict resolution.[4][6] Core principles include self-managed learning, community responsibility, and rejecting deficit-based views of children.[6][8]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in the early 1990s as a self-managed alternative school, it received provisional primary school approval in 1996, full recognition as an Ersatzschule in 2000, and Secondary Level 1 approval in 2015, enabling Berufsbildungsreife (BBR), erweiterte BBR (eBBR), and Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA) exams.[5] Owned by the nonprofit Freie Schule Pankow eG (cooperative of staff and parents), it serves ~120 students from Berlin districts and surroundings, with a multidisciplinary team of educators, social pedagogues, craftsmen, interns, and FSJ volunteers.[1][4][5] The new building at Grabbeallee 66, Berlin-Pankow (13156), supports freies Lernen; hours are 8:30-16:15.[1][4][7] Previously in Weissensee.[1]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** and Grund- und Sek1-Schule (primary and secondary levels 1), it covers grades from primary (Einschulung) through 7-10, focusing on freies Lernen where students decide learning timing and topics, with adults providing frameworks.[1][2][8] Offers state-equivalent BBR, eBBR, and MSA; Abitur requires transfer (partners with Anna-Freud-Oberschule).[8] Multilingual website (German, English, Spanish, French); no specific curriculum details beyond self-directed, community-developed daily routines.[4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Emphasizes hands-on, self-directed activities including **ausflüge (excursions)**, nature, music (musisches), sports, crafts (handwerkliches), workshops, and project weeks.[4] Features Lernräume (learning spaces), Werkstätten, Schulregeln co-developed with students, Elternmitarbeit (parent involvement), and events like Winterfest, ball court construction, "Grünes Klassenzimmer." [4] Daily communal encounters build trust and independence.[8]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
State-recognized Ersatzschule since 2000 (primary) and 2015 (Sek1), member of BFAS and daks, aligning with their 2021 #bildungistpolitisch statement.[4][5] No awards, rankings, or specific outcomes mentioned; focuses on equivalent state exams (BBR, eBBR, MSA).[8]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Process**: Three steps—(1) 1-page application letter from parents answering 3 questions (wishes for child, why this school, expected learning processes) plus child's learning reflection, sent by post/email; prefer post-Infoabend/Tag der offenen Tür attendance; deadlines: Erstklässler by Nov 15 (prior year), 7th grade SEK1 by Jan 24, 2025 (for 2025/26, extended contextually); Quereinsteiger anytime (currently no spots).[9] (2) Kennlerngespräch (get-to-know interview).[9] (3) Kennenlerntage (trial days, e.g., Jan 20-22 or Feb 17-19 for 2024/25 starters), followed by mutual decision.[9] Capacity ~120; no current free places.[1][10]

**Fees**: No specific tuition, registration, material, meal, or after-school costs mentioned on pages; "Finanzen" section listed but empty in scrape. School seeks donations (e.g., GLS Bank DE16 4306 0967 1161 8869 00, betterplace.org), implying needs-based funding.[4] No scholarship or income-based details; one PDF noted but unrelated to fees.[4]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Jüdische Traditionsschule combines secular and Jewish education, fostering natural curiosity, joy in learning, and personal development in a warm, trusting environment where every individual feels unique and valued.[Scraped Website Content] Its philosophy emphasizes a strong Jewish foundation aligned with Jewish life, festivals, and customs, alongside Berlin's state curriculum, promoting self-confident personalities responsible in society, with values of care, compassion, tolerance, and multilingual communication in German, Hebrew, and English from Grade 1.[Scraped Website Content][1] The school prioritizes small classes, year-group-spanning learning, individualized support, lifelong learning, and competencies in subject knowledge, methods, social skills, and personal growth.[Scraped Website Content][6]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded around 2004 (with over 20 years of operation by recent accounts, starting with 20 children from its Kita), the school has grown to over 300 students and includes the Harry Schwarzer Chabad Grundschule and Gutman Chabad Gymnasium; the Gymnasium opened in 2010/2011.[Scraped Website Content][3][5] Located in Berlin's Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf district at Westfälische Str. 15, 10709 Berlin (one source notes a prior or alternate address at Spandauer Damm 220), it serves an international community of students from around the world in a state-approved Ersatzschule (substitute school).[School Information][Scraped Website Content][3][7] Facilities include computer-supported teaching, well-equipped state sports halls and swimming pools for physical education, and kosher meals; it operates full-day from 7:30 to 15:40 with after-school activities.[Scraped Website Content][1][4]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an ISS-Gymnasium (private substitute school), it covers Grundschule (primary, from age 5) through Gymnasium (secondary), following Berlin's Rahmenlehrpläne and Stundentafel for public schools, offering equivalent qualifications including Abitur after G8 (8 years).[School Information][Scraped Website Content][2][3] Key features: small learning groups, differentiated instruction, year-group-spanning classes, computer-aided lessons, early English from Grade 1 (playful, integrated themes), Hebrew and English as profile subjects with increased hours (4 weekly each from Grade 7, plus Hebrew in Jewish Religion/Judaistik), strengthened German (5 hours), Jewish studies, and extracurricular learning; Leistungskurse (advanced courses) available by choice.[Scraped Website Content][1][2][3][Curriculum Page] Focus areas include language (speaking/listening, reading/writing), math (forms, numbers, measurements, data), and competencies like self-perception, social living, nature, history, and media.[Curriculum Page]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students experience full-day care (7:30-15:40), small supervised classes from age 5, physical education ("Erziehung durch Bewegung") in state sports facilities to build independence and creativity, creative free-time activities under professional guidance, and international community fostering tolerance.[Scraped Website Content] Extracurriculars include off-site learning activities, scholarships, daily morning prayers, creative celebration of Jewish festivals, kosher meals, smartboards, online teaching, and tutoring for individual strengths/weaknesses.[Scraped Website Content][1][3] No specific arts exchanges, sports competitions, or travel mentioned.[2]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
State-approved Ersatzschule offering public-equivalent degrees (Abitur, etc.), with ~60 teachers/educators (recently ~40+ plus rabbi) supporting over 300 students; no awards, rankings, or specific outcomes like university placements detailed.[Scraped Website Content][5] Emphasizes high-level education enabling Gymnasium-to-Abitur progression in a Jewish atmosphere.[1][3]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
No admissions process, requirements, or fee details (tuition, registration, materials, meals, after-school, scholarships beyond general mention) provided on scraped pages; no PDFs with fee info found. Scholarships are noted as available, but specifics absent.[Scraped Website Content]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Jüdisches Gymnasium Moses Mendelssohn (JGMM) and Rabbinerin-Regina-Jonas-Schule (JONAS) are state-recognized private schools of the Jüdische Gemeinde zu Berlin, emphasizing lived Judaism through mandatory Hebrew and Jewish religious studies for all students, regardless of faith. They serve as places of learning and communal life, fostering tolerance, acceptance, and integration amid differences in belief, origin, and ability, with a values framework rooted in Jewish tradition (Massoret), human dignity (Kwod haBrijot), and lifelong learning (Limmud), opposing antisemitism, racism, and violence while promoting differentiated instruction.[2][6]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1862 at Große Hamburger Str. 27 in Berlin-Mitte, the school merged boys' and girls' institutions in 1931, was closed by Nazis in 1942, and reopened in 1993 in its historic listed building (modernized with a glass tunnel attic by architects Kay Zareh and Ruth Golan), renamed JGMM in 2012, with JONAS added in 2023/2024. It enrolls about 550 Jewish (70%) and non-Jewish (30%, mostly Christian or non-confessional) students in classes capped at 24 (fewer at JONAS), offering kosher meals daily (meat or vegetarian, supervised by Rav Ehrenberg, included in fees), modern digital boards in all classrooms, Microsoft 365 licenses since 2020, stationary computers/laptops, and a sekretariat open weekdays (until 15:45, Fridays 14:00).[1][2][3][4]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
JGMM is an ISS-Gymnasium (integrated secondary school) from grade 5 (gymnasial track) through Oberstufe (qualification phase to Abitur), with JONAS as integrated Sekundarschule grades 7-10; Hebrew is obligatory grades 5-10 (modern Hebrew to B/C levels, with catch-up for transfers), plus Jewish religion/philosophy (mandatory Grundkurs in Oberstufe, electable as Leistungskurs). Other languages: English (S1), French (2nd/3rd from grade 7, DELF certifiable), Russian (AG, Oberstufe Leistungskurs/S3); Leistungskurse include art, biology, German, English, geography, history, Jewish philosophy, math, Russian. Features teilgebundener Ganztagsbetrieb, digital integration, and state recognition as Ersatzschule; graduation via Abitur (Gymnasium), MSA possible.[1][2][5][7][8]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
All students participate in Jewish holidays, kosher lunches, and religion/Hebrew classes; extracurriculars include Schnellschach team (recent win), "Quiz der Religionen," Gedenken events, Schulsanitätsteam, Moses Journal (Winter/Pessach 2025), Oberstufe trips, Konflikt AG, Mediation/Streitschlichter, Russisch AG. Digital tools (Teams, Promethean boards) and computer curriculum enhance learning.[2][7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Schnellschach team success highlighted; first post-Holocaust Jewish Gymnasium in Germany leading to Abitur; no specific rankings, awards, or test outcomes detailed in content.[2][9]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Enrollment Process**: Annual intake: one grade 5 class (max 24 students), two grade 7 Gymnasium classes (max 24 each, requires grade sum ≤14 in German/Math/English from grades 5-6 or probe class), max 14 for Sekundarschule grades 7-10. Open Day: 18 January 2026 (11-15 Uhr). Required docs: application form, school reports/prognosis, motivation letter (handwritten by student), child ID/vaccination/Berlinpass. Quereinstieg possible if places available (esp. grades 7-8 with French/Russian; Hebrew catch-up required); Oberstufe needs MSA. Apply via online form to info@jgmm.de or Jugendförderung (for contracts).[2]

**Fees**: No exact EUR amounts listed on scraped pages (Fees/Tuition page empty, no PDFs with fee info). School contract and payments via Jüdische Gemeinde's Dezernat für Bildung und Jugend (Oranienburger Str. 28-31, tel. 030/880 28-133); kosher meals included in Schulgeld. No registration/material/after-school/scholarship details provided.[2]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
No explicit mission statement or detailed educational philosophy is extracted from the scraped website content, as main, about, and curriculum pages were empty. The school is described as a christlich geprägte (Christian-oriented) and state-recognized private school under the Erzbistum Berlin's sponsorship since 1970, emphasizing high-quality academic education combined with religious, political, and cultural formation open to all regardless of faith or background.[3][4]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded on February 8, 1947, by Sr. Luminosa Wimmer as an all-girls Gymnasium with 70 students; operations began November 4, 1947, initially staffed largely by nuns.[1] Expanded in 1953 with a Realschulzweig; first Abitur in 1954; granted state-recognized private school status in 1955.[1] Grundschule (primary school) inaugurated June 10, 1964, by Erzbischof Alfred Bengsch, with a branch in Märkisches Viertel opening January 1971.[1] Sponsorship transferred to Erzbistum Berlin in 1970.[1][3] Currently serves about 700 students from areas including Waidmannslust and Märkisches Viertel.[3][4] Located idyllically at Tegeler Fließ in Berlin-Reinickendorf (Waidmannslust).[3] Ongoing development includes new two-field sports hall and school cafeteria starting 2024/2025.[4] Recognized as excellent environmental schools (Gymnasium and ISS); primary school launched energy-saving program in 2023.[4]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Operates as an **ISS-Gymnasium** (Integrierte Sekundarschule and Gymnasium), including a Grundschule (primary school) and its Märkisches Viertel branch.[1][4] Recent addition of an 11th grade in ISS to enable transition to Gymnasium.[4] Subjects include Religion, Geschichte (history), Mathematik, Latein, Biologie, Physik, Geografie, Englisch, Musik, Informatik, Deutsch, Sport, Französisch, Kunst, Chemie, and specialized programs like Schülerfirma (student company), BSO, WAT, Schulgarten/Naturwerkstatt (school garden/nature workshop), and DS (likely Deutsch als Zweitsprache).[8] Leads to Abitur via Gymnasium pathway.[1] No details on MSA or BBR from content.

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
No specific extracurriculars, sports, arts, or exchanges detailed in scraped content. School features a Naturwerkstatt/school garden and student performances (e.g., "Wir sind eins" by DS course).[6][8] New sports hall and cafeteria under construction.[4] Community group "Gemeinschaft Christlichen Lebens" (GCL) active since 1987.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Gymnasium and ISS certified as **ausgezeichnete Umweltschulen** (excellent environmental schools).[4] State-recognized private school since 1955.[1] Continuous growth with increasing demand.[4] Part of Erzbistum Berlin's 26 schools serving 8,600 students.[4]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
No admissions process, requirements, or fee details extracted from scraped website content (fees/tuition and admissions pages empty; no PDFs found). As a state-recognized private school, it may have fees, but none specified here.[1]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Katholische Schule Sankt Franziskus (KSSF) is a Catholic school under the sponsorship of the Archdiocese of Berlin, emphasizing Christian values, community, and spiritual integration in education. The school begins each day with a morning school prayer and holds regular Gottesdienste (services), fostering a sense of togetherness and reflection, as seen in events like the Patronatstag with a festive service at St. Matthias Church.[1][2][10] It promotes an integrated, differentiated approach in an Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) with gymnasialer Oberstufe, using binnendifferenzierung (internal differentiation) in subjects like Math, German, English (Grundniveau/Erweiterungsniveau), and sciences from grade 9, while keeping class units intact to support diverse learners without grade repetition risks.[1][6][7] The philosophy highlights practical competencies through open learning, project-based work, Lerntechniken, and berufsorientierung (career orientation), alongside Montessori principles in the Grundschule.[3][7]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1889 as a "Katholische Höhere Mädchenschule" in Berlin-Schöneberg, the school was taken over by the Franziskanerinnen order in 1918, closed by Nazis in 1940, and reopened post-war; the main building, a listed monument designed by Margot Zech-Weymann, was inaugurated in 1959 by Cardinal Döpfner.[2] It became co-educational gradually (boys from 1959, full in Grundschule by 1967) and adopted its current ISS form in 2008 with gymnasial Oberstufe reopened.[2][5] Located at Hohenstaufenstr. 1-2, 10781 Berlin (Tempelhof-Schöneberg district), it serves a diverse community of ~750 students from over 25 nations, promoting peaceful Christian coexistence.[6] Facilities include a school building with Schulhof (playground), offene Kapelle (open chapel), Schulpastoral, Textilwerkstatt (textile workshop on 6th floor), Mensa (catered meals), Schulkiosk, and proximity to St. Matthias Church and local parks like Gleisdreieckpark.[1][scraped Main Page][scraped About Page]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a private **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** with Grundschule (two-stream, Montessori-influenced Verlässliche Halbtagsgrundschule) and gymnasialer Oberstufe up to grade 13, it covers grades JÜL (Eingangstufe) to 13, with classes like 1Md-6Md, 7a-d, 8a-d, 9a-d, 10a-d, 11a-c, 12, 13.[1][3][5][7][scraped Admissions Page] Differentiation occurs in core subjects from grade 7 (Math/Deutsch/Englisch: GR/ER levels; sciences from 9/10); Wahlpflichtfächer (electives) include Französisch/Spanisch (grade 7), then Art and Design/Drama/Histographie/Informatik/Naturwissenschaft/Sport/WAT (grade 9).[7][9] Languages: Englisch (S1), 2./3. Fremdsprachen (Latein/Spanisch/Französisch).[9] Unterrichtszeiten span up to 18:05, with structured pauses and grade-specific Essenspausen (meals for grades 5/6/7/8/9/10).[scraped Curriculum Page] Graduation pathways: **BBR** (Berufsbildungsreife), **eBBR** (erweiterte), **MSA** (Mittlerer Schulabschluss) after grade 9/10; transition to Oberstufe for **Abitur/Fachabitur** after 13 years.[5][7][8] Career prep includes Praktika, BIZ visits, Girls/Boys-Day from grade 7-10.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in rich extracurriculars emphasizing arts, sports, projects, and outings: Adventskonzert (joint Grund-/Oberschule event at St. Matthias); Patronatstag; Sport Wahlpflicht excursions across Berlin; Gemeinschaftsspiele in Gleisdreieckpark; music projects like "Mythen und Legenden" with RIAS-Kammerchor at UdK; "Franziskus Voices" choir at Berliner Schulchorwettbewerb (FEZ Berlin); Upcycling Nähen (Bauchtasche); Goldschmiedin workshop (Art/Design); Känguru-Mathematikwettbewerb; history trips (AlliiertenMuseum, Marienfelde Notaufnahmelager, Barberini Museum Potsdam, Afro-Germans walking tour Schöneberg); bilingual AI short stories (9a); VR sexuality education ("Virtual S*Exploration"); Sozialpraktikum (grade 11, Suppenküche).[scraped Main Page][1] Weekly Box-AG, Schüler:innenparlament, diverse electives, and community service like Weihnachtsmarkt for social projects enhance experiences.[1][scraped Fees Page]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No specific awards, rankings, or certifications are detailed in the content, though participation in competitions like Känguru-Mathematikwettbewerb (31st edition, ~880,000 participants nationwide) and Berliner Schulchorwettbewerb indicates active involvement; first Abitur in 2011 after Oberstufe reopening.[scraped Main Page][2] The school supports progression to Abitur, with ongoing Leistungsstand feedback.[7]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions involve an Aufnahmeantrag flyer (PDF available); process references Förderverein (support association) with homepage www.foerderverein-kssf.de.[scraped Fees Page] No exact fee amounts (monthly/annual tuition, registration, materials, meals, after-school) are stated on scraped pages; all Schulgeld info directs to https://schulen-erzbistumberlin.de/schultraeger/schulgeld (Archdiocese site) or the 2019 Aufnahmeantrag PDF: http://kssf-berlin.de/neu/wp-content/uploads/2019/07/Flyer_mit_Beileger_2019_Endversion16.1.2019.pdf (may contain details).[scraped Fees Page] No scholarship or income-based options mentioned. Mensa meals are catered (no costs specified).[1]
