description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Katholische Schule Sankt Marien emphasizes education in a **Christian spirit**, promoting modern teaching, independent work, and talent development for every student.[5][1] The school supports parents in **christlich orientierte Wertebildung** (Christian-oriented values education), maintains a religions-sensitive approach to all students' beliefs, and fosters ethical standards based on a Christian view of humanity that respects individuality and aims for maturity and responsibility.[10][4] It unites an Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) and Gymnasium under one roof, focusing on holistic development through teaching, learning, and community life.[5]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
The Integrierte Sekundarschule has operated as a three-track secondary school since the 2011/12 school year.[7] Located in **Berlin-Neukölln** at Donaustr. 58, 12043 Berlin, it is a private Catholic school under the Archdiocese of Berlin, led by **Schulzentrumsleiterin Frau Rahner (Theresa Rahner)**.[1][5][6] Facilities include a **cafeteria**, two computer rooms, laptops, whiteboards, and a 360° virtual tour; a kiosk offers snacks.[1][6][5] It features bound full-day operations (GGB), serves as a state-recognized substitute school, and includes a primary school section (Grundschulteil) in the school center.[1][6] Community ties involve the Förderverein, Bildungsspender support, and the Archdiocese's online network.[5]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school offers **ISS (Integrierte Sekundarschule, grades ~5-10)** and **Gymnasium (grades 5-12/13, including Oberstufe)**, leading to **Abitur** via Gymnasium pathways; it supports Zweiter Bildungsweg (second educational path).[1][2][5][6] **Languages**: English (S1/S4), second foreign languages (French/Polish/Latin from grade 7), third (Latin/Spanish/French from grade 8); DELF prep, language trips (France, Spain, Poland), English/Polish competitions.[1][2][6] Special profiles: **kunstbetontes Profil** (art-focused, split classes in middle school), **theaterbetontes Profil**, **MINT-classes** (Math, IT, Science, Tech; e.g., 7/8Ga Mint-Forscherprojekt).[1][2][6] Differentiation from grade 7 (Math/English), grade 8 (German), grade 9 (sciences); remedial in German/Math, homework supervision.[1] Full-day ISS (mandatory, ends 15:30, Thursdays 13:45); detailed schedule from 8:00-17:00.[Curriculum Page] Leistungskurse in Oberstufe: Art, Biology, Chemistry, German, English, Geography, French, History, Math, Music, Political Education.[6] Grade 10 course choices cover linguistic-artistic, social sciences, MINT fields.[2]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
**Extracurriculars** include choir (e.g., Christmas performances), guitar lessons, chess, Catholic Student Youth, student company (e.g., Advent market), school newspaper (NIF insights), social projects (MISEREOR, soup kitchens, Third World aid).[1][2][5] **Sports**: Rugby, volleyball, basketball, soccer, strength training.[1][6] **Social/Community**: Mediators, school medical service, **Sozialpraktikum** (3-week in grade 10/11), class trips (e.g., 10Gb), Advent wreath/festivals.[2][5] Arts/theater emphasis; political education, career orientation.[1][6]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Student **Aanmihan Colin Cowdry** won a prize from the Deutsche Physikalische Gesellschaft.[Main Page] MINT research projects and competitions in languages noted; no broader rankings or certifications detailed.[1][2][5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Process**: Open to all children/youth; decision by school leadership after interview with student/parents.[10][Admissions Page] For **grade 7 (ISS/Gymnasium, 2026/27)**: Schedule interview from 13.10.2025 (tel: 030-39881620) or open day 11.10.2025; use online application.[Admissions Page] For **grade 11**: Informal written application (email, fax 030/398816230, mail) with last 2 report copies.[Admissions Page] Downloadable docs: Rahmenschulordnung, contract termination, fee suspension request.[Admissions Page]  
**Fees**: No exact amounts listed on pages; refer to Archdiocese link (https://www.erzbistumberlin.de/bildung/katholische-schulen/schulgeld-und-ordnungen) with Vorab-Schulgeldrechner (preliminary fee calculator); PDFs available: "INFO Schulgeld Eltern Infoblatt.pdf" (175 KB), "Elterninformation Online-Anträge_Schulgeld.pdf" (134 KB), fee suspension form.[Admissions Page] Possible income-based options/suspensions implied; no specifics on registration, materials, meals, aftercare, scholarships.

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Königin-Luise-Stiftung's mission is encapsulated in its leitbild: **"Selbst sein - Miteinander - Weiterkommen"** (Be oneself - Together - Progress forward), emphasizing individuality, respectful social interaction, and fostering competence through demanding yet supportive education.[1][7] It promotes individual strengths and diversity ("Selbst sein"), a positive school climate through respect, dialogue, conflict resolution, and responsibility ("Miteinander"), and performance-oriented learning with self-directed methods, creativity, physical activity, and technology integration ("Weiterkommen").[1] The foundation blends traditional values from its classical roots with modern facilities, aiming for a culture of peace, environmental protection, sustainability, and global equity as a UNESCO-Projektschule.[1][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in **1811** as a "living monument" to Queen Luise, influenced by reformers like Delbrück and Humboldt, it evolved from a girls' institute into a co-educational campus offering schools and boarding for over 200 years.[1][3] The community includes **ca. 950 students**, **170 staff**, and **33 boarding places** (noted as 56 in some external sources) in a family-like environment across Grundschule, Sekundarschule, Gymnasium, and Jugendhilfe-Internat.[1][4] The campus in Berlin-Steglitz-Zehlendorf (Podbielskiallee 78, opposite the Botanical Garden) features school buildings, boarding areas, sports fields, playground, park, bio-certified kitchen, and specialized rooms (e.g., science labs, music/art rooms, workshops, computer labs).[1][3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school offers a continuous pathway from primary to upper secondary: **Grundschule** (grades 1-6, homogeneous classes plus mixed-age Montessori 1-4); **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS-Gymnasium)** (grades 7-12, enabling all Berlin qualifications: Berufsbildungsreife, Mittlerer Schulabschluss/MSA, Fachgebundene Hochschulreife, or **Abitur** after grade 12/13); **Gymnasium** (grades 5-12, performance-oriented with low class sizes of 18-23).[1][2][3][5][6][7] Languages follow **E-F-Sp** (English from grade 3/5, French, Spanish); differentiated instruction in core subjects; religions (Protestant/Catholic); broad upper-secondary courses including Philosophy, Film, Digitale Welten; no BBR mentioned.[2][3][4][6] Open all-day program with projects, homework help, electives.[2]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Experiences emphasize small classes (18-22 students), personal greetings by principals, prevention programs for social skills, inclusion via internal/external support, and family-like boarding in three homes.[1][2][6] Extracurriculars include AGs (working groups) in music, theater, sports, social learning, Hauptstadt-Aktivitäten (capital city projects), foreign partnerships, tandem projects; ganztagsbetreuung (all-day care) with homework, projects, electives; bio-meals with daily raw veggies/fruit, feedback options, special diets at no extra cost.[1][2][3][4]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Certified as **UNESCO-Projektschule** for peace, environment, sustainability, and equity projects; **Internationale Nachhaltigkeitsschule - Umweltschule in Europa** for "Bildung für nachhaltige Entwicklung."[1][3] Gymnasium Abitur average slightly above Berlin average; supports solid to top performers via broad courses/small groups.[6] State-recognized private school with ongoing quality focus.[4][9]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions require an online form selecting school type (Grundschule, ISS, Gymnasium, Internat), with child's name/grade, parents' details, desired entry time, cover letter, last two report cards, behavior assessments (PDF/DOC up to 10MB); contacts per section (e.g., Cornelia Otto for Grundschule).[1] Income-based tuition via annual application (by July for new year, effective next month; max rate without application); sibling discounts; reductions for social aid (e.g., §7 Lernmittelverordnung: welfare, housing aid); contacts: Michelle Zurek, Daniel Russau.[1] **No exact tuition amounts listed on pages**; external source notes **extern: income-dependent, max €360/month**; **intern: €1,787.43/month** plus extras (trips, sports); meals included in operations but bio-certified DGE-standard at no extra for specials.[1][4] No registration/material/after-school fees specified; scholarships via income scale.[1]

description:
# Montessori Campus am Tegeler Forst - School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

**Montessori Campus am Tegeler Forst** is a **private Montessori school** operating under the pedagogical principles of Maria Montessori.[5] The school emphasizes child-centered learning through the Montessori method, which focuses on individual development and self-directed activity within a prepared environment. While the website content provided does not include an explicit mission statement, the school's commitment to Montessori pedagogy is evident in its curriculum structure and educational approach across all grade levels.

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Location & Setting:** The school is located in Berlin's Reinickendorf district at Gabrielenstraße 12-18, 13507 Berlin, positioned near the Tegeler Forst (Tegel Forest).[5][6] This natural setting provides opportunities for outdoor learning and environmental education.

**Campus Structure:** Montessori Campus am Tegeler Forst operates as a comprehensive educational facility with multiple divisions, including Montessori Kinderhäuser (early childhood centers) and school programs spanning primary through secondary education.

**Community Engagement:** The school offers campus merchandise through an online shop (Wear Together partnership) and actively recruits staff, including opportunities for federal volunteer service (Bundesfreiwilligendienst) and voluntary social year positions (Freiwilliges Soziales Jahr).

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grade Levels Offered:**
- **Grundschule (Primary School):** Grades 1-6
- **Sekundarschule I (Secondary School):** Grades 7-10
- **Early Childhood:** Montessori Kinderhäuser (preschool/kindergarten)

**Educational Approach:** The school implements **Montessori pedagogy** as its core curriculum framework.[5] The method emphasizes graduated development, individual pacing, and hands-on learning materials.

**Admission Windows:**
- **Grade 1 (Primary):** August 1 - October 31 annually for the following school year
- **Grade 7 (Secondary):** August 1 - February 12 annually for the following school year
- **Mid-year transfers:** Lateral entry (Quereinstieg) available for grades 1-6 and 7-10

The website does not specify language programs, international curriculum options, or specific graduation pathways (Abitur, MSA, BBR).

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

The provided website content does not include detailed information about extracurricular activities, sports programs, arts offerings, or student exchanges. The school's proximity to Tegeler Forst suggests nature-based learning opportunities, consistent with Montessori environmental education principles.

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

No awards, rankings, certifications, or specific achievement data are mentioned in the available website content.

## 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES

**Enrollment Process:**
- Prospective families submit an **Anmeldebogen (registration form)** via email or postal mail
- Non-binding pre-registration (unverbindliche Vormerkung) is available
- Contact: Sekretariat, Frau Lunenburg | Phone: 030/68405070 | Email: sekretariat@montessori-tegel.de

**Tuition Structure (Income-Based):**

| Program | Monthly Cost Range |
|---------|-------------------|
| **Grundschule (Grades 1-6)** | €70 - €375 |
| **Sekundarschule I (Grades 7-10)** | €90 - €500 |

**Financial Assistance:** Tuition reductions are available upon request with documentation of financial circumstances (Auf Antrag kann unter Darlegung der wirtschaftlichen Verhältnisse eine Ermäßigung gewährt werden).

**Additional Fee Information:** A detailed fee schedule (Beitragsordnung) is available but not fully displayed on the main website. The document "Beitragsordnung-gueltig-ab-240801.pdf" (valid from August 1, 2024) contains comprehensive fee information.

**Note on Open House:** As of the current date, all open house (Tag der offenen Tür) appointments are fully booked. The next opportunity to visit the school is scheduled for spring 2026.

schulname: PepperMont Freie Sekundarschule für ganzheitliches Lernen
description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
PepperMont Freie Sekundarschule für ganzheitliches Lernen operates under Pfefferwerk Stadtkultur gGmbH, emphasizing holistic, child-centered learning inspired by Maria Montessori pedagogy. The approach prioritizes self-directed learning (**Freiarbeit**), individual needs, interests, and personal progress over traditional note-based assessment, with feedback provided in real-time and detailed annual reports; grades are issued only if required for secondary school transitions. Diversity is viewed as enrichment, with jahrgangsgemischten (mixed-age) groups of ~23 children per class (grades 1-3 and 4-6), led by teams of two teachers and one educator, fostering mutual help, varied skill levels, and holistic development beyond intellectual work.[About Page]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 2002 as **Freie Grundschule Pfefferwerk**, it became a state-recognized alternative school (**staatlich anerkannte Ersatzschule**) in August 2007 and functions as a full-day open-form school (**Ganztagsschule in offener Form**). Located at Greifswalder Str. 88, 10409 Berlin (Pankow district), it serves children primarily from Pankow and Mitte districts but accepts students from all Berlin areas and Brandenburg without catchment restrictions. The school opens at 7:30 AM, lessons start at 8:30 AM, and children may leave at 3:00 PM or stay for after-school care (**Hort**) managed by Kinder-Kinder Berlin e.V. Currently five learning groups, expanding to six by August 2021; business office at Christinenstraße 18/19, 10119 Berlin.[About Page][School Information]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Primary school (**Grundschule**) for grades 1-6 in mixed-age groups, aligned with Berlin's framework curriculum but driven by children's interests and needs. Core Montessori-influenced methods promote self-determined learning joy, with significant time for **Freiarbeit**; no routine grades, but detailed progress reports. No explicit mention of languages, special programs like Abitur/MSA/BBR, or secondary pathways in scraped content; related Pfefferwerk entities offer Fachschule für Sozialpädagogik (vocational training).[About Page][Main Page]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Private Goethe-Grundschule aims to provide high-quality education through a dedicated school concept and regulations, emphasizing continuous improvement in both quality and quantity.[2] It promotes trilingual instruction as Berlin's first German-Russian-English primary school, fostering knowledge, competencies, and pride in attendance while stressing close parent-teacher collaboration for student success.[1][2] The philosophy highlights shared goals with parents, teacher competence, and open communication to overcome challenges and prepare children for future pathways, particularly gymnasiale or sekundäre tracks.[2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Established as a state-recognized private primary school (Grundschule) under Bifiz gGmbH (copyright since 2014), it operates in Berlin-Reinickendorf at Wittenauer Str. 112, 13435 Berlin, with recent expansion to a new location.[1][2] It is part of a network including Kita Kalinka, Kita Edelweiss, Goethe Gymnasium, and TSV-Sportuniversum – Berlin 2019 e.V., serving a community focused on trilingual families.[2] Facilities mentioned include those for all-day care (Hort), art education (e.g., Bode-Museum visits), and sports; specific infrastructure details like classrooms or playgrounds are not detailed.[2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an **ISS-Gymnasium** primary school (Grundschule, grades 1-4 typically), it offers a trilingual program in German, Russian, and English, open to children with or without prior language knowledge in English/Russian.[1][2] The curriculum supports gymnasiale or sekundäre pathways, with state recognition (12P07 code).[1][4] No explicit details on Abitur, MSA, or BBR pathways, but it positions itself for higher secondary progression; mid-year entry uses placement tests in German, Math, English (and Russian if applicable).[2]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include art lessons at Bode-Museum, charity initiatives ("Kinder helfen Kindern"), sports via TSV-Sportuniversum, and cultural events like Sommersonnenwende celebrations.[2] All-day care (Hort) is available, with gallery/videos documenting activities from 2009-2015 (e.g., school enrollments, aid packages to Mogilev).[2] Open Door Day (11.10.2025) offers tours and teacher talks; community engagement via parent info and events.[2]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
**Staatlich anerkannte** (state-recognized) status as Berlin's first German-Russian-English Grundschule.[1][2] Documented activities include expansions, charity (190 packages to Mogilev), and media coverage (Presse section), but no awards, rankings, or specific academic outcomes like test scores or university placements are mentioned.[2]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions welcome interest via online/PDF forms; open to prior kindergarten attendees (e.g., Kita Kalinka) or others with/without English/Russian basics.[2] If demand exceeds spots, selection based on social/fachliche competencies; requires school contract. Mid-year entry via placement tests (Deutsch, Math, Englisch, optionally Russisch), with possible extra support.[2] Contact: sekretariatgoetheschulenberlin@gmail.com; Open Door Day 11.10.2025 at 11:00 Uhr.[2]

**Fees**: Income-staffelt (tiered by family income), with discounts for 2nd/3rd child, low-income families, or non-working parents. Requires 3-month pay stubs or last year's tax notice pre-contract. No exact EUR amounts listed on pages; Anmeldeformular PDF may contain details (fee-related: False per scrape, but check for updates).[2]

For families earning EUR 17,965-30,000, the standard monthly fee is EUR 100.00 (EUR 1,200 annually). For higher incomes, fees range from EUR 200-800 monthly depending on family gross income. Sibling discounts apply: 25% for 2nd child, 50% for 3rd child, 75% for each additional child. Families with incomes under EUR 30,000 receive reduced fees and adjusted additional charges. One-time fees at enrollment include: registration/administration fee EUR 25.00 (EUR 15.00 for families under EUR 30,000 income), library use EUR 10.00, craft materials EUR 35.00, copy costs EUR 20.00, maintenance EUR 100.00, and e-class book EUR 25.00. Admission test fee EUR 15.00. Security deposit (Kaution) of two months' tuition required. Lunch is free for grades 1-6 during school year; EUR 3.40 per meal for grades 7+. Free lunch places reserved for 20% of class seats for low-income families. Hardship exemptions and temporary income reduction accommodations available upon written request.

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's mission is to foster, focus, and differentiate by promoting individual potential in grades 7-13, awakening interests, developing skills, and ensuring successful school completion, whether MSA or Abitur.[1][2] Core values emphasize individualized support regardless of primary school recommendations, preparation for a globalized society through future-oriented foci like economics, English, and psychology, and a pedagogical credo of nurturing young people's abilities while creating perspectives.[1][2] The educational approach features performance-differentiated instruction, engaged teachers, two social pedagogues, a mediation team, and variable use of new media.[1]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
No specific founding date is mentioned; the school operates under the Stiftung Private Kant-Schulen gGmbH, part of a network with 6 locations, over 2250 students, and 70+ nations represented, emphasizing a familial atmosphere and reliable parental exchange.[2][4] Located at Körnerstr. 11, 12169 Berlin (Steglitz-Zehlendorf district), with a primary school branch at Körnerstr. 49; accessible with parking for disabled, wheelchair elevator, and WC.[5][7] Facilities include modern specialized rooms, computer workstations, school library, KANT Bistro (student café as economics project), in-house prepared hot lunches with fresh fruit/salad/vegetables, and student study hours (SAS) for homework.[1][2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
An integrated secondary school (Integrierte Sekundarschule, ISS) with gymnasial upper secondary (grades 7-13), offering state-recognized substitutes (BBR, MSA after grade 10, Abitur after 3-year upper secondary per VO-GO).[1][2][5] Follows internal curriculum based on Berlin framework plans; English as first foreign language required, with options like French/Spanish (grades 7-8), Biology/Geography/Sports (7-8), Economics/IT (9-10); unique Berlin offering of Economics in English (basic/advanced courses).[1][2] Special emphases: Economics (business/economics topics, project-based, internships in grades 9/11), English, Art, Psychology; performance-differentiated classes, career orientation, Betriebspraktika.[1][2] Bilingually oriented Deutsch/Englisch offers.[5]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Bounded all-day school (08:00-16:00/17:30 for SEK II) with no cancellations, including supervision, AGs (e.g., mediation, IT, cooking, art, workshop, soccer, basketball, volleyball, table tennis, school café, band, newspaper, chess, sailing, graffiti), projects, excursions, annual class trips, ERASMUS project, sports tournaments.[1][2][6] Career events, internships (grades 9/11), mediation for harmonious climate, SAS for deepening material.[1][6]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
State-recognized substitute school with officially acknowledged BBR, MSA, Abitur; unique Economics in English program in Berlin.[1][2] No specific awards, rankings, or exam results detailed in content.[5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Enrollment Process:** Submit application form with copies of last three summer report cards to school secretariat; invited to personal interview with principal (Seidel, Tuomas), who decides on placement; requires promotion certificate to target grade and English as first foreign language; positive impression and "chemistry" with parents valued.[1][5] Contact: +49 30 79000360, info@kant-oberschule.de.[5][9]

**Fees:** No specific tuition, registration, material, meal, or after-school costs mentioned on scraped pages. PDFs likely contain details: Anmeldeformular (https://www.kant-oberschule.de/wp-content/uploads/sites/5/2023/01/Aufmahmeantrag-KOS-Stand-01-2023_V.1.0.pdf), Aufnahmeantrag SEK II (https://www.kant-oberschule.de/wp-content/uploads/sites/5/2023/01/Aufmahmeantrag-SEKII-Stand-01-2023_V.1.0.pdf). No scholarships or income-based options noted.[1]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Berlin International School is an IB World School and officially recognized private school in Berlin, operating as a private, non-profit, non-denominational day school that provides student-centered learning for international and local students from Grade 1 through university entrance preparation.[3][4] It is part of Stiftung Private-Kant Schulen gGmbH and emphasizes the IB Learner Profile values, offering challenging programs to realize academic and individual potential, a local-global curriculum with inquiry focus, development of thinking/communication/social skills, multilingualism (especially English and German), independent/teamwork/leadership opportunities, technology innovation, effective assessment, and enhancement of social/intellectual/physical/creative competencies.[3][4] The ethos promotes well-rounded, curious, compassionate learners through academic excellence, global citizenship, personal growth, multilingualism, critical thinking, and student agency in an inclusive community.[6]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1998 by Horst Seidel (founder of Private Kant Schulen) and Andreas Wegener at the request of the Berlin Senate to serve the international community amid the government relocation from Bonn, the school started with 20 students and 12 teachers, growing to serve 996 students from over 70 nationalities by 2023.[1][3][6] It is governed by the non-profit Stiftung Private-Kant Schulen gGmbH (SPKS), part of the Kant Foundation, which operates other schools; multiple Kitas are managed by sister organization Kant-Kindergarten gGmbH.[3] The student body is diverse, with ~45% German (many with dual nationality or study abroad experience) and the rest from 70+ nationalities.[3] Facilities include Campus Dahlem with school buildings, a purpose-built Kita, and two multi-purpose halls.[3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school offers education from kindergarten through Grade 12 (start Grade 1, end Grade 12), predominantly in English with German as alternate language, serving all German proficiency levels.[3][5][6] It is an authorized IB World School since 2000 for IB Diploma Programme (DP, exams in May, coeducational day school) and since 2003 for IB Primary Years Programme (PYP).[5] Middle years include Cambridge IGCSE (UK-IGCSE) and Berlin state qualifications; DP subjects offered include English A Lit/B, German A Lit/Ab Initio/B, French/Spanish B, History, Geography, Chemistry, Environmental and Societies, Sports Exercise Science, Theory of Knowledge, plus languages like Urdu/Hindi/Japanese A Lit.[5][6] Programs emphasize inquiry-based learning, bilingual (English-German) education, and preparation for university.[6]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
The school facilitates opportunities to enhance social, intellectual, physical, and creative competencies inside and outside the classroom, including leadership experiences, teamwork, and technology integration.[4] Specific extracurriculars, sports, arts, or exchanges are not detailed in the provided content.

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school celebrated its 25th anniversary in 2023, highlighting 25 years of milestones, challenges, and achievements serving a diverse international community.[1] It holds IB World School authorization (PYP since 2003, DP since 2000) and official recognition as a private school by the State of Berlin; membership in CIS (Council of International Schools).[3][5] No specific awards, rankings, or student outcome data (e.g., graduation rates) are mentioned.

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
No specific enrollment process or requirements are extracted from the content. No fee information (tuition, registration, materials, meals, after-school care, scholarships, or income-based options) is provided in the scraped pages; no PDFs with fee details were found.[3]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's mission is to ensure all youth in Germany, regardless of social or cultural background, have access to a school qualification and seamless transition to further education or work.[Main Page][8] It emphasizes **chancengerechtigkeit** (equity of opportunity) through individualized and language support, relationship and family work, career orientation, intercultural learning, and behavior management to equip students for successful, fulfilling lives.[Main Page] The educational approach focuses on a proven concept fostering better chances for school completion and job entry, with a family-like atmosphere, trust-building relationships, and enthusiasm-driven teaching.[Main Page][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 2014 by Quinoa Bildung gGmbH (a social enterprise named after the resilient quinoa plant), the school started in Berlin-Wedding's Soldiner Kiez (a socially challenged area) in a former post office on Osloer Straße, later moving to Kühnemannstr. 26, 13409 Berlin (Gesundbrunnen, old Wedding, Bezirk Mitte).[1][2][4][7][6] It serves ~150 students from socioeconomically weak or migrant families, mirroring the local demographic, with 28 teachers and 3 school social workers led by Pantelis Pavlakidis (and others).[3][9][6] Facilities include renovated spaces post-2025 flood (walls opened, wiring/pipes/floors/ceilings updated) and climate adaptation measures funded by BMUV (e.g., heat/drought/heavy rain resilience via GreenAdapt).[Blog/About Page] An open house is scheduled for 14.01.2026 (16:00-18:00).[Main Page]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a state-recognized private **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS)** for grades 7-10, it offers pathways to: **berufsorientierter Abschluss (BO)** for students with special needs, **Berufsbildungsreife (BBR)**, **erweiterte BBR (eBBR)**, **Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA)**, and **MSA with upper secondary eligibility (MSA GO)**.[4][7] Programs stress language promotion, intercultural learning, digitalization, tutoring, family/parent work, career orientation ("Fach Zukunft"), and relationship work; no Abitur mentioned.[Main Page][7]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students describe a family-like environment with trusted teachers and individual care.[Main Page] Experiences include berufsorientierendes projects like "Bauhütte" (2024: 5-day site redesign with partners like Baufachfrau Berlin e.V.), internships (e.g., Spanish student's marketing internship), and climate adaptation initiatives.[Blog/About Page] Intercultural focus reflects diverse home languages (most non-German).[2] No specific sports, arts, or exchanges detailed.

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Since founding, **Abschlussquoten exceed state neighbor schools in Berlin-Mitte by ~20%** (e.g., >90% average; 100% in 2024 for 10-year anniversary cohort vs. 30-50% local dropout rates).[Main Page][3][8] 2024 highlights: strong social cohesion, high student satisfaction.[8] Recognized for equity work; state-approved Ersatzschule.[7] Blog notes proven success in completions and job entry.[Main Page]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Enrollment starts with contacting the secretariat (info@quinoa-bildung.de, +49 30 983224100), followed by info evenings/open house, parent-child meetings on rules/daily life, and trial day.[6][Main Page] Targets grades 7 entry for local demographic.[2][6] **Fees**: No exact amounts listed on pages; historically low/nominal (most of 24 inaugural students paid none, 3 paid small amount via private funders) or income-based for disadvantaged families; no registration/material/meal/aftercare specifics found (no PDFs with fees).[2][5] Scholarships/subsidies implied via social model and donors.[Main Page]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Christburg Campus operates as a free carrier of state-recognized Christian schools and kindergartens in Berlin, with **Jesus at the center** of its work. Its mission is to enrich Berlin's educational offerings professionally and competently on a biblical foundation, helping children and youth grow into strong personalities aligned with the divine image of humanity and discover their callings.[3][7] Core values derive from Christian faith, emphasizing trust in God, parental responsibility, value transmission beyond competencies and knowledge, individual support without writing off students, and creating safe learning environments where Jesus is experiential, guided by the Holy Spirit.[1][3] The approach integrates state curricula with a Christian perspective, fostering purposeful living, societal engagement, and partnerships.[3][6]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1998 with the Corrie-ten-Boom-Schule starting as a Realschule with 18 students (state-recognized since 2002), the campus expanded to its current site in 1999—a protected Wilhelminian school ensemble at Christburger Str. 14-14B, Prenzlauer Berg, owned since 2005.[2][6] It now serves ~900 students across three kindergartens (e.g., Bunte Burg, Helles Nest, Immanuel-Kita), three primary schools (Elisabeth-Abegg-Grundschule, Immanuel-Grundschule), and the Corrie-ten-Boom-Schule (integrated secondary school).[1][2][3] Locations include Prenzlauer Berg (Pankow district), Spandau, and Hellersdorf; the Prenzlauer Berg site houses ~300 secondary students in bright rooms, with a familial atmosphere praised by parents for personal greetings, events, and small size where everyone knows each other.[2][7] Facilities feature historical brick buildings, school workshops for crafts, digital blackboards, and open all-day care (OGB) at primary level; no homework supervision is offered.[1][4][5][6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The campus delivers state-recognized alternative education fulfilling all state learning goals, supplemented by Christian perspectives, from kindergarten through grade 13.[1][3][6] Primary schools (Grundschulen like Elisabeth-Abegg and Immanuel) offer English as first foreign language, open all-day care, year-group learning groups, and school entry phases.[5] The Corrie-ten-Boom-Schule is an integrated secondary school (ISS, grades 7-13, ~25 students/class) with English (1st foreign language) and Spanish (2nd); Sekundarstufe I leads to **Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA)** after grade 10, Sekundarstufe II to **Abitur** in 3 years (gymnasial upper level since 2017).[1][4][6] Supports include free remedial classes, individual/group tutoring, learning plans, training, presentation/prüfung prep, peer tutoring, counseling, talent development, and BerlinPass partnerships; second educational pathway and vocational prep (application training, internships, job agency cooperation).[1][4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars emphasize community and creativity: school festivals, performances, charity runs (Spendenlauf), summer festivals, Advent markets, Christmas services, baking, art projects (e.g., Seerosen like Monet, wood fish crafting), self-defense courses, garden trips, smartphone awareness, 48-hour prayer events, and student bands (e.g., Christmas song).[7] Sports/practical skills via workshops and internships; arts in regular classes (e.g., grade 6b creativity); excursions (e.g., August-Heyn garden school, neighborhood cleanups); home learning workshops; AG groups (project weeks).[1][4][7] Parental testimonials highlight patience, family atmosphere, and events fostering belonging.[7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Students achieve **above-average results** vs. other Berlin schools: nearly all pass MSA after grade 10, with high gymnasial recommendations; first Abitur cohort planned for 2020.[1][6] Awarded **Fair-Family-Gütesiegel** in 2014 for family engagement.[2] Strong individual support yields high success rates; ~900 students across sites with state oversight.[2]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
No specific enrollment process details (e.g., applications, requirements) are provided in the content. **No tuition, fees, or cost details** (monthly/annual, registration, materials, meals, after-school, scholarships, income-based options) appear on scraped pages or listed PDFs (none found). As a private state-recognized school, ~2/3 costs are state-funded; remainder from parents/donors.[2] Contact Sekretariat@Christburg-Campus.de or visit www.christburg-campus.de for inquiries.[4][5]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
SIS Swiss International School Berlin aims to develop students as **global citizens** with strong awareness of their roots through a bilingual, stimulating learning environment that fosters academic excellence, enjoyment in learning, and pride in achievements.[1][2] Core values include **lived bilingualism** (equal use of German and English in instruction and daily life), independence, value-orientation (honesty, respect, responsibility), a full-day school community with structured programs, and performance-oriented diversity.[1][2] The philosophy centers students' learning needs, emphasizes international orientation with local roots, continuous education from kindergarten to university entrance, technology-enhanced learning (e.g., digital tools, programming from early stages), and a vibrant, diverse community.[1][2][5]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
SIS Berlin originated in **2016** when SIS took over Internationale Schule Villa Amalienhof in Germany.[5] It is part of the SIS Group, a pioneer in bilingual education since 1999, operating 20 schools worldwide (Switzerland, Brazil, Germany, Italy) with 4,844 students from 102 nationalities and 1,070 staff; SIS is a joint venture of Klett Group (Germany) and Kalaidos Education Group (Switzerland).[1][2][6] The school is privately financed by fees and state subsidies, politically/ideologically independent, and serves a diverse international community.[1][3] Located at **Heerstr. 463 (entrance at Reimerweg 11), 13593 Berlin (Spandau district)**, with office hours Monday-Friday 8 a.m.-3 p.m. and phone +49 30 3643 9820.[1] No specific facilities details are provided beyond a full-day setup with meals, homework supervision, and flexible care.[1][2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
SIS Berlin offers continuous bilingual (German/English) education from **kindergarten through primary school, secondary school (Years 7-10, state-recognized), and upper secondary (Years 11-13, state-approved)**, culminating in **German Abitur** and **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**.[1][2][3] Specific offerings include **Kindergarten, Grundschule (primary), Sekundarschule, Gymnasiale Oberstufe, and IBDP**; technology integration starts early (programming, media, e-safety).[1][2][5] IBDP authorized since **March 9, 2016** (IB code 051071, English instruction, coeducational day school).[3] Primary/secondary (Years 7-10) meet German compulsory attendance; equals state schools.[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
The school provides a **ganztägige Schulgemeinschaft** (full-day program) with block lessons, midday meals, supervised homework, flexible morning/afternoon care, courses, and holiday programs.[1][2] A **Student Exchange Programme** is available group-wide.[2] Technology enriches learning across subjects.[2][5] Community features a "lebendige Schulgemeinschaft" (vibrant school community) with international diversity, alumni networks, and events like open days.[1][2] No specific sports, arts, or extracurricular details extracted beyond general full-day offerings and positive parent reviews on respectful environment and language clubs.[4]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
SIS Berlin is an **IB World School** for Diploma Programme since 2016.[3][5] Part of SIS network accredited for IB at multiple sites (e.g., Basel, Zürich); participates in international benchmarking like ISA.[5] State-recognized private primary/secondary (Years 7-10), state-approved upper secondary.[1] Group serves 4,844 students from 102 nationalities; positive parent feedback on motivation, bilingual progress, and community.[2][4] No specific awards, rankings, or outcome stats (e.g., Abitur/IB scores) mentioned.

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions involve enrollment ("Einschreibung") with open days/information evenings for families to learn about curriculum and community; contact via info.berlin@swissinternationalschool.de.[1][2] No detailed requirements extracted. **No specific fee amounts** (tuition, registration, materials, meals, after-school) are listed in provided content; "Preisliste" (price list) pages noted but empty. PDFs with potential fee info: none found. School financed by fees and state subsidies.[1]

### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The **TING-Schule** operates as a democratic school emphasizing "Ich entscheide!" (I decide!), with core principles of **independence, personal responsibility, self-directed learning, and genuine participation**. The weekly school assembly serves as the central pillar of the school community, inspired by the historical meaning of "TING" as a people's and court assembly. It is an **Integrierte Sekundarschule (ISS) with Grundstufe** (integrated secondary school including primary level) in private sponsorship by Freie Demokratische Schule Berlin e.V., promoting self-determined daily school life.[1][3][6]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
No specific founding date or history is mentioned in the content. The school community includes **approximately 50 students aged 5 to 17** who shape their school day democratically, supported by **seven permanent staff members**. Leadership includes Svenja Weiß and others like Dr. Eckhard Krug and Anne Viezens. It is located at **Berliner Str. 21, 13189 Berlin (Pankow district)**, with some sources noting variations like Berliner Straße 100 or Grosse Seestrasse 16, and alternative addresses like Berliner Allee 128 or Berliner Allee 128.[1][3][6][8] No facilities details (e.g., classrooms, playgrounds) are provided.[3]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school follows a **self-determined learning model** in an **open-form all-day school (Ganztagsschule in offener Form)** with **mixed-age learning groups (jahrgangsübergreifende Lerngruppen)** across grades, including Grundstufe (primary) and secondary levels up to potentially 7-10. Foreign languages offered are **English (1st), French (2nd), and Japanese (3rd)**. It aligns with Sudbury school concepts and is a **state-approved replacement school (genehmigte Ersatzschule)**. No specific graduation pathways (e.g., Abitur, MSA, BBR) or curriculum details are explicitly stated; enrollment targets ages **5-13** year-round, serving students up to 17.[1][3][4][6]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in **self-determined daily routines** through democratic participation and weekly assemblies. **Open all-day care (Offene Ganztagbetreuung)** is available. No mentions of extracurriculars, sports, arts, or exchanges.[1][3][6]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No awards, rankings, certifications, test results, or outcome data (e.g., graduation rates) are mentioned in the content.[3][6]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Admissions**: Contact via email (ting@ting-schule.de) with phone number for prompt response; year-round enrollment for ages **5-13**. First-graders' process starts no earlier than **January of the school obligation year**. Phone: 030-53215656 or 0171-8198389.[3][9]  
**Fees**: No tuition amounts, registration fees, material fees, meal costs, after-school care costs, scholarships, or income-based options are mentioned on any pages. No PDFs with fee information found.[3]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Wilhelmstadt Schulen, operated by IBEB gGmbH, emphasizes individualized student development, comprehensive general education, and values-based personality growth, with a focus on fostering each student's unique strengths and viewing weaknesses as opportunities.[1][3][4][5] Core principles include respectful interaction, mutual appreciation, collective learning, openness, friendliness, and helpfulness among students, teachers, and parents; the Gymnasium holds the "Friedensschule" title for student engagement in peace initiatives.[1][5] The campus promotes high-level education for children from diverse backgrounds and languages, living by the motto "Wir leben Bildung" (We live education).[4][7]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded on a former military barracks site in Spandau's Wilhelmstadt, the Gymnasium opened in 2004, followed by the Realschule (now Oberschule) in 2006, Grundschule in 2014, and two Kitas; a new Kita for over 200 children is under construction.[1][3][4][7][9] The campus serves ~290-352 culturally diverse students across schools (Gymnasium: grades 7-12 with ~290-322 students; Oberschule: ~130-352 students), taught by ~28 international teachers per school, with small classes (~24 students) enabling personalized support.[1][2][4][5][8] Facilities include a green campus, school cafeteria offering breakfast and fresh lunches, upgraded science labs for experiments and robotics, and spaces for projects.[4][7]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As an ISS-Gymnasium (integrated secondary school with upper secondary level), it offers continuous grades 7-12 leading to Abitur, alongside Oberschule pathways to MSA and potentially BBR; Grundschule starts English from grade 1.[1][2][3][4][5][6] Curriculum follows Berlin's state frameworks, with multilingual support (English, plus Turkish, Spanish, Russian, French); electives include robotics and "Jugend forscht"; weekend exam prep and holiday courses boost outcomes.[2][4][5] Admission considers grades (5th/6th grade), primary school recommendation, social behavior, and child interviews.[2][5]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include diverse AGs (working groups) like silk painting, sports, graffiti, chess, community trips, Red Cross first aid training, and "Schüler mit Verantwortung" projects.[2] Events feature neighborhood festivals, World Cleanup Day, open monument day, summer festivals, Tanzania school projects, career fairs, and sports like "Jugend trainiert."[Main Page] International achievements include award-winning participation in VILIPO – Vilnius International Project Olympiad.[Main Page] Open-door policy allows anytime access for students/parents.[5]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Abitur success rate is 99.3-100%, with a recent average grade of 2.2 and nine students scoring 1.0-1.2; MSA pass rate nearly/fully 100% via Saturday prep.[4][5][Main Page] Recognized as "Friedensschule"; high individual support yields strong preparation for university, apprenticeships, and careers.[1][2][5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admission to Gymnasium relies on 5th/6th grade grades, Grundschule recommendation, social behavior assessment, and child interviews; Oberschule considers grades and personal suitability beyond notes.[2][5] As a private school (staatlich anerkannte Ersatzschule), no fee details are provided in the content; no PDFs with fees found, and dedicated fees/admissions pages are empty.[Main Page][School Information]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
Berlin Cosmopolitan School's mission is **empathy, equity, excellence in education**, striving for innovation by challenging the traditional education system while fostering individual support, bilingual education, and preparation for university or apprenticeships.[Main Page][4] Core values include diversity, social justice, equal opportunities, and inclusion regardless of background, with a focus on nurturing talents in arts, sciences, sports, and social engagement.[Admissions Page][2] The philosophy emphasizes modern pedagogy, outdoor learning in nature (integral for over six years to promote independent thinking and sustainability), multilingualism, and drawing from Montessori, Reggio Emilia, and IB approaches.[Main Page][Curriculum Page][2]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in **2004** as Berlin's first bilingual English-German kindergarten with 18 children, it expanded to primary school in 2007 and secondary in 2009, becoming an IB World School in 2013.[3][5] Now a non-profit with ~700 students from over 45 countries, it promotes a diverse, international community as active world citizens through strong academics, social skills, and intercultural development.[1][2] Facilities include **City Campus** (Rückerstr. 9, 10119 Berlin-Mitte, a 1912 listed brick building near Alexanderplatz, with SMARTboards, dance hall, outdoor basketball/football pitch, and additional rooms in Rückerstraße/Mulackstraße)[Main Page][6][5]; **Nature Campus** at Mügelsee (with new digital weather station for real-time data on temperature, pressure, humidity, rainfall, wind; data accessible via app/website for hands-on learning in math/science/geography).[Main Page] Other features: Cosmovatorium (music/dance school, first in Berlin for IB Dance), alumni site, and hiring for international team.[Main Page][5]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Offers **Kindergarten/Preschool** (from age 1), **Primary**, and **Secondary (Gymnasium)** through grade 12, as a recognized **IB World School** with Primary Years Programme (PYP) and Diploma Programme (DP) on Berlin's framework; primary language English, bilingual English-German.[1][3][4] Focus on sciences, music, dance, nature, digitalization, core subjects; Bilingual Abitur (German qualification for German/international universities, with 3 written/oral exams + presentation) and Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA) in grade 10 (Berlin state exam; English Science option for recent arrivals, plus English oral/presentation; prerequisite for upper school).[Main Page][4] Individualized support via Child Support Team for academic/social-emotional needs, including classroom collaboration, morning support (reading/math/science), referral processes, and external specialist links.[Curriculum Page]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Emphasizes outdoor/nature-based learning, workshops (e.g., Emotions for grade 4), language trips (grade 9), internships (grades 7/8/10), trial weeks (Schnupper Woche for grade 10 Abitur/DP), poetry readings on identity/multilingualism, democracy discussions (e.g., Venezuela at Berlin Freedom Week).[Main Page] Extracurriculars include music/dance (Cosmovatorium, IB Dance), sports (outdoor pitches), after-school programs, winter vacation programs, life skills workshops (stress management, conflict resolution, self-acceptance), and real-world projects like weather station data analysis.[1][Curriculum Page][Main Page] Multilingual events, alumni networking, and menu plans support daily life.[Main Page]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Authorized **IB World School** (PYP/DP since 2013); first Abitur class (2016) achieved Berlin's best average grade of **1.4**; first IB Diploma graduates in 2015; first MSA exams passed in 2013; state recognition for secondary school in 2014.[3][5] Students regularly graduate with top Abitur grades; publicly available weather data highlights practical science engagement.[Main Page][2]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Enrollment process**: Online application form (complete for login/status; non-editable post-submission); requires photo (passport-style JPEG/PNG), sole custody proof if applicable; **50 EUR non-refundable fee** for primary/secondary (not kindergarten); documents for "APPLIED" status; open to diverse talents/backgrounds, with scholarships for need.[Admissions Page][Fees Page] School uniforms compulsory (via Dress for School or Cosmo Swap second-hand; includes sportswear). **Tuition**: Income-based (detailed schedule in PDF: https://www.cosmopolitanschool.de/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Gebuehrenuebersicht-Schulgebuehren-2024-25-.pdf); sibling discounts; scholarships for financially needy.[Fees Page] No exact EUR amounts listed on pages (e.g., no monthly/annual/material/meal/after-school specifics); refer to PDF for 2024/2025 details.[Fees Page]

description:
# Phorms Berlin Mitte - Comprehensive School Profile

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

**Mission Statement**
Phorms Berlin Mitte operates as a **bilingual German-English immersion school** that combines personal development with academic excellence[1]. The school's founding concept emphasizes "Persönlichkeitsentwicklung mit Leistungsorientierung" (personality development with performance orientation).

**Core Educational Values**
The school's philosophy rests on five pillars[1]:

- **Bilingual immersion**: Continuous German-English education from age two through Abitur using the immersion method with native-speaking teachers
- **Future competencies**: Project-based learning, critical thinking, creative problem-solving, and responsible digital media use
- **Child-centered approach**: Pedagogically appropriate class sizes with optimal teacher-student ratios enabling individual support
- **Performance and personality**: Applied skills development alongside personal growth
- **Global perspective**: "Local Schools – Global Education" motto emphasizing international and open-minded worldview

The immersion method allows children to acquire language naturally through daily use across all subjects rather than through traditional language lessons[2].

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Founding and Growth**
Phorms Berlin Mitte was **founded in 2006** and celebrated its **20th birthday in summer 2025**, making it the oldest and largest of all Phorms schools[7]. The school serves approximately **950 students** across all levels (kindergarten through secondary school)[1], with the primary school alone housing **450 children**[3].

**Location and Accessibility**
The campus is located in **Berlin-Mitte district at Ackerstr. 76, 13355 Berlin**, in an attractive red-brick building in central Berlin[7]. The location offers optimal positioning near the Berlin Wall Memorial and Humboldthain park, with excellent public transportation access[1].

**Facilities and Infrastructure**
The modern campus includes[1]:

- Three modern sports halls
- High-quality science laboratories
- Specialized music rooms for practical music-making
- Spacious library
- Theater hall with fully equipped stage, lighting, and sound system
- Smartboards in all classrooms
- Lockers with USB connections in the secondary school
- Green spaces including Gartenplatz nearby

**Community Character**
The school emphasizes a **familial, international atmosphere** with students, teachers, and parents from diverse backgrounds[1]. The campus operates as a full-day community from early morning through 6 PM, integrating learning and living harmoniously[1].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grade Levels and Structure**
The school offers continuous education across three divisions[1]:

- **Kindergarten (Kita)**: From age two
- **Primary School (Grundschule)**: Grades 1-4
- **Secondary School (Gymnasium)**: Grades 5-13, leading to Abitur

**Bilingual Curriculum**
All instruction occurs at native-speaker level in both German and English[7]. The curriculum follows the Berlin state curriculum while incorporating extended content in English-language subjects[5].

**Primary School Subject Distribution**
The primary school integrates bilingual instruction across all subjects, with language learning embedded naturally throughout the day rather than confined to dedicated language lessons[2].

**Secondary School Subject Distribution**
In middle school (Grades 5-6), subjects are divided as follows[4]:

- **Taught in German**: German, Art, History, Sports
- **Taught in English**: English, Mathematics, Science, Geography
- **Additional offerings**: Extra lessons in Social Studies and Science

In upper school (Grades 11-13), students specialize in subjects of interest with advanced courses available in[4]:

- English, History, Biology, Mathematics, Art, Geography, German
- Music ensemble course for interested students

**Language Programs**
Starting in Grade 7, students choose an additional foreign language: **Spanish or French**[4]. Students develop subject-specific vocabulary in both German and English with ease through the immersion method[4].

**Graduation Pathways**
Students earn the **state-recognized Berlin Abitur** supplemented by a bilingual component in applicable subjects[5]. The school is state-recognized, ensuring qualifications are recognized both in Germany and internationally[5].

**Special Programs**
From Grade 5, the school offers a **bilingual science-focused secondary school** for students already enrolled at Phorms who demonstrate strong academic drive[4]. From Grade 7, a bilingual secondary school pathway is available for both Phorms students and external applicants[5].

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Extracurricular Activities**
From Grade 3 in primary school, children can select different **elective subjects**[6]. The secondary school (from Grade 7) offers diverse elective choices in language and science areas[6].

**Afternoon and Enrichment Programs**
The school provides an **extensive and varied afternoon program** with working groups, free play, and situational offerings that extend classroom learning[6]. A **varied vacation program** offers opportunities to deepen personal interests beyond regular instruction[1].

**Individual Support and Advancement**
In the secondary school, the school provides[1]:

- Targeted support during "Studytime"
- Additional remedial instruction
- **Rotation model (Drehtürmodell)** for highly motivated learners
- Active participation in competitions

**Language Support**
For multilingual students needing additional support, the school provides **tailored remedial programs** with individual lessons in German (DaZ - Deutsch als Zweitsprache) and English (ESL - English as a Second Language) matched to each child's skill level[3].

**Special Needs Support**
The school supports children with special educational needs who study in regular classes alongside other students[6].

**Cognitive Benefits**
Research cited by the school indicates that bilingual learners develop[3]:

- Better language and foreign language skills
- Enhanced creativity
- Improved language awareness
- Greater cognitive flexibility

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

**Academic Performance**
The secondary school has **consistently achieved results well above the state average** for over 14 years with highly qualified teachers[5].

**Certifications and Recognition**
- **State-recognized private school** (staatlich anerkannt)
- Qualifications recognized both in Germany and internationally[5]
- Bilingual Berlin Abitur with extended content in English subjects[5]

**Curriculum Alignment**
The school follows the Berlin curriculum while offering extended content in English-language subjects to provide deeper and broader education[5].

## 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES

**Admissions Process**
The enrollment process consists of three steps[1]:

1. **Online registration**: Complete the application form online without obligation
2. **Personal meeting**: School contacts families to discuss next steps, answer questions, and provide insight into daily operations
3. **Placement offer**: School notifies families via email of acceptance and sends contract documents for digital signature

Admissions can occur mid-year if spaces become available[1].

**Fee Structure**

The school implements **income-dependent parental contributions** to enable access for families across different economic backgrounds. Fees are calculated based on total family annual income.

**Kindergarten/Reception (Kita) Fees**

Monthly parental contributions (Förderbeitrag) are income-based:

- Up to €20,000 annual income: €135/month
- €50,000+: €212/month
- €75,000+: €246/month
- €100,000+: €275/month
- €130,000+: €349/month

These amounts are rounded and do not include meal costs and other fees[1].

**Additional Costs**
- **Meal costs**: Separate meal allowance (Verpflegungspauschale) applies
- **Berlin Kita voucher**: Covers part of basic costs; families must apply at their district office (Bezirksamt) at least two months before enrollment
- **Förderverein membership**: Monthly contribution required to support the bilingual concept and enhanced teacher-student ratios

**Sibling Discounts**
Families with multiple children receive reductions[1]:

- 1st child: Full monthly contribution
- 2nd child: 25% discount
- 3rd child: 50% discount
- 4th and subsequent children: 75% discount

**Tax Deductibility**
Parental contributions and Förderverein fees may be deductible as childcare expenses (Betreuungsaufwendungen) in tax returns under §10 Abs. 1 Nr. 5 EStG, up to two-thirds of expenses (maximum €4,800 per child annually), excluding instruction and extracurricular activities[1].

**Primary and Secondary School Fees**

The website content provided does not include specific tuition amounts for primary school (Grundschule) or secondary school (Gymnasium). A fee schedule document (Beitragsordnung) is referenced but the specific amounts are not displayed in the accessible content.

**Contact Information**
- **Phone**: +49 30 318 737 010 (Monday-Friday, 10:00-16:00)
- **Email**: admissions.berlin@phorms.de
- **Admissions Coordinator**: Hannah Vollmer (Kita)

description:
# School Profile: Phorms Berlin Süd

## 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY

Phorms Berlin Süd emphasizes **bilingual education as an integral part of everyday school life**, with the goal of developing students who speak German and English fluently at near-native level[4]. The school's educational philosophy centers on fostering **independent learning, problem-solving, and reflective thinking** through creative learning methods and interdisciplinary team and project work[4].

The school promotes **intercultural skills and global awareness**, guiding students to understand cultural and linguistic diversity while recognizing global connections[4]. A key principle is supporting individual strengths through early identification of abilities and targeted fostering through remedial lessons and enrichment initiatives[6]. The school describes itself as providing "Local Schools – Global Education," combining rigorous academics with personal development[6].

## 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES

**Founding and Development**

Phorms Berlin Süd was **founded in 2008** and opened its primary school that year[5]. The secondary school opened in 2010[3]. The campus celebrated its 10th anniversary in 2018[3].

**Location and Facilities**

The school is located in **Steglitz-Zehlendorf**, Berlin's green southwestern district[5][6]. The campus occupies a historic building with significant heritage: it was formerly the Telefunken works, where tens of thousands of patents were developed in the 1930s, and later served as headquarters for the American Berlin Brigade after 1945[3]. The building was extensively renovated in 2009 before the school moved in[3].

The campus includes **sports facilities with two sports fields with running tracks, as well as throwing and long jump facilities**[5]. The location combines urban cultural access with suburban green spaces and parks[5][6].

**Community Size**

The campus serves approximately **700 children and students** across nursery, primary, and secondary levels, with over 120 educators[5].

## 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS

**Grade Levels and Structure**

The school serves students from **age one through secondary school graduation**, with programs in nursery, primary school, and secondary school[6].

**Bilingual Immersion Approach**

Teaching takes place using the **immersion method** in both German and English, with native-speaking teachers in each language[5][6]. The bilingual concept is maintained from nursery through Abitur graduation[5].

**Academic Pathways**

Students are guided to the **Abitur in 12 years**[4]. The school offers an **advanced English course in upper secondary** where students can earn the **Cambridge Advanced English Certificate**, an internationally recognized qualification[4].

**STEM Focus**

The school has a **strong STEM profile** and is recognized as a "House of Little Researchers"[6]. The curriculum emphasizes applied skills, responsible media use, and development of critical thinking and problem-solving abilities[6].

**Curriculum Foundation**

The curriculum is based on the outline curriculum of the federal state of Berlin[2].

## 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES

**Learning Methods**

The school implements **"rhythmized lessons"** in primary school, where longer teaching units alternate with time for projects and shorter learning units, with flexible lesson endings rather than fixed bell schedules[5].

**Enrichment and Support**

The school identifies and fosters individual talents through remedial lessons and the **"Digitale Drehtür" (Digital Revolving Door) model**[6]. An **engaging afternoon program and various competitions** enhance the learning experience[6].

**Extracurricular Activities**

While specific extracurricular programs are not detailed in the available content, the school emphasizes sports facilities and cultural offerings available through Berlin's location[5][6].

## 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES

**Certifications and Recognition**

- **Official recognition as "Ersatzschulen"** (state-recognized private schools) for both primary and secondary school[3]
- **"Schule mit Courage, Schule ohne Rassismus"** (School with Courage, School Without Racism) award in 2016[3]
- **Cambridge Advanced English Certificate** program established, with first exams in 2017[3]
- **AP (Advanced Placement) exams** offered, with first exams in 2016[3]
- **Abitur graduation** program established, with first graduates in 2016[3]
- **Vocatium partner school** designation in 2018[3]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Evangelische Schule Frohnau emphasizes a continuous educational pathway from primary school through gymnasium, rooted in evangelical Christian values, fostering a familial atmosphere, responsibility for others, and an open worldview. Evangelical religious education is a regular subject, with traditions of Bible interpretation promoting love, freedom, and community life; the school aims to make students "weltkundig" (worldly aware) by understanding global realities, competing values, and Christian life models.[1][2][4][6] Key elements include morning circles, class councils, devotions, services, and festivals as integral to school life, alongside active anti-mobbing efforts through the "Faire Schule" initiative and social internships.[4]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in the tradition of 1948 evangelical schools emerging from the Confessing Church's resistance to National Socialism, the school is owned by the Evangelische Schulstiftung EKBO (Evangelische Kirche Berlin-Brandenburg-schlesische Oberlausitz) and serves over 700 students in a green, idyllic setting in Berlin-Frohnau, Reinickendorf district.[1][2][3][4][6] It operates as a state-recognized substitute school (Ersatzschule) with qualifications equivalent to public schools, led by Christine Behnken, featuring two parallel classes per year (zweizügig) from grades 1-12 for stability.[2][3][4][7] Facilities include accessibility features like parking for disabled persons, wheelchair-accessible elevator and WC, and after-school care (Hort); it supports a democratic culture with conflict mediators, active student representation, and ecological initiatives like a bicycle check day.[1][7]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
The school offers a continuous track from Grundschule (grades 1-4, or up to 6 in some structures) to Gymnasium (up to grade 12/Abitur), with two streams per yeargang, year-group learning in early grades, and fachlehrkraft principle (subject teachers) from grades 5-6.[4][5][7][9] Languages include English from grade 1, optional strings from grade 2, and third languages French or Latin; elective profiles like S1 English/French, S2 English/Latin.[5][7] Evangelical religion is mandatory; upper levels include gymnasiale Oberstufe leading to Abitur, with no mention of MSA or BBR pathways.[2][4][7]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Students engage in diverse extracurriculars including Arbeitsgemeinschaften (AGs) like strings class, junior orchestra, choir, dance, theater, handball, Champions-Runner, upcycling, and sports courses; grade 5-6 projects in brass, choir, art, and performing arts culminate in joint performances.[1][4][5] Additional experiences feature class trips (grades 3 and 6), school partnerships, social internships, Klassenrat, morning circles, festivals, and anti-mobbing programs; after-school Hort couples support hours with play and learning.[4][5]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school has received the Fobizz-Siegel (renewed for 2026) for exceptional commitment to digital education and teacher training.[4] It promotes a democratic school culture with high student satisfaction, conflict mediators, active student council supported by communication seminars, ecological responsibility (e.g., Umweltamt, Fahrradcheck-Tag), and global learning initiatives.[1]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions for grade 1 start after summer holidays of the prior year (e.g., 9-19 September 2025, 8-12 Uhr), with phone appointments for interviews; grades 5/7 after winter holidays.[4] Required documents: application form, last two report cards (original/copy), birth certificate copy, measles vaccination proof (original), baptism certificate if applicable, and school assessment for grades 5/7; waitlist requires updated certificates by 15 September.[4] As a private school in free sponsorship, monthly tuition (Schulgeld) is income-based per the EKBO Schulstiftung table (details on carrier's site); no exact EUR amounts listed on pages, but PDFs may contain forms (not fee-related per metadata).[4] No specifics on registration, material, meal, or after-school fees; contact Lisa Gutjahr for queries.[4] Newsletter and parent contributions mentioned, but no scholarship details.[4]

Exemption for recipients of SGB II, SGB XII, AsylbLG upon proof (Hort and meals still payable). No specific amounts or table in provided content; refer to carrier's site. description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Evangelische Schule Köpenick (ESK) is committed to a **Christian worldview** that values, accepts, and respects the individuality of each person, providing education and upbringing grounded in the Gospel.[1][3] It emphasizes being **engaged, social, and creative**, fostering holistic learning through "heart and hand" – thinking, feeling, and acting – with high value on creativity, curiosity about other cultures, solidarity, empathy, tolerance, and environmental stewardship.[1][3] The school's leitbild promotes Christian life via worship, rituals, and festivals; individualized learning paths; preparation for self-determined lives; and close parent partnerships.[3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in **2005** by an initiative of engaged parents, ESK is a state-recognized substitute school (Ersatzschule) owned by the **Schulstiftung der Evangelischen Kirche Berlin-Brandenburg-schlesische Oberlausitz (EKBO)**, led by Michael Tiedje since 2017.[1][2][3] Located at **Grüne Trift 169, 12557 Berlin** in the quiet Wendenschloss area of Treptow-Köpenick district, between the Dahme, Spree rivers, and Müggelberge, it serves **611 students** (2021/2022) with **61 teachers**.[1][2][3] The campus features DDR-era buildings (1953-1963), including a three-story main building with basement, art rooms in a northern wing, and a gym hall; a digital school tour is available.[1][2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a **Gymnasium** (grades 5-12/13), ESK follows Berlin's state curricula and frameworks, offering a musisch-künstlerisches (arts-focused) profile with subjects including **Religion, Deutsch, Darstellendes Spiel, Mathematik, Chemie, Biologie, Physik, Musik, Kunst, Spanisch, Französisch, Latein, Englisch, Geschichte/Politik, Sport, Geografie, Informatik/ITG**.[1][6][9] Languages: English/Französisch from S1, Englisch/Spanisch from S4, Latein as 3rd.[6] Upper school (Oberstufe) leads to **full Abitur** after 12 years, with Leistungskurse options like Deutsch, Englisch, Französisch, Spanisch, Mathematik, Biologie, Physik, Kunst, Musik, etc.[4] Leistungsbewertung applies grades 5-10; open to evangelical primary transitions and others.[1][3][4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
ESK promotes holistic, creative learning with arts emphasis (kunstbetontes and musikbetontes Profil), including Darstellendes Spiel, Musik, Kunst.[1][3][6][9] Extracurriculars include Freiwilligendienste (e.g., FSJ), Schülervertretung, Jahrgangsteams, Berufsberatung, Oberstufenberatung, Vertretungsplan.[1] Community initiatives: "Schule ohne Rassismus – Schule mit Courage" (since 2011), "Faire Schule" certification (2013) for global justice and sustainability ("global denken – lokal handeln").[4][5] Partnerships, Gottesdienste, rituals, and environmental efforts enhance social experiences.[3]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
ESK reports strong **Abitur results**: 2024 average **2.0**, 2023 **1.9**, 2022 **1.8**, 2021 **1.9**, 2020 **1.8**, 2019 **2.01** – all above Berlin Gymnasium average and top in Köpenick district.[5] Certifications: "Faire Schule" (epiz, 2013); "Schule ohne Rassismus – Schule mit Courage" (since 2011, part of Germany's largest school network with 1000+ schools).[4][5] State-recognized Ersatzschule with equivalent qualifications.[3]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Admissions**: Open for Quereinstieg in grades 9-11 (contact Sekretariat).[1] For grade 5/7: Submit copies by **01.12.2025** (application form, Zeugnisse from grade 1/3, Geburtsurkunde, Taufurkunde if available, Passfoto, engagement certificates); by **31.01.2026** (Halbjahreszeugnis grade 4/6, Förderprognose, Sec I Anmeldung).[1] Grade 5: Optional info talks **10.01.2026, 11-15 Uhr**; grade 7: Mandatory tests **14.01.2026 & 20.01.2026, 16-17 Uhr** (register from 10.11.2025).[1] If Förderprognose <2.2 average, Senate probeunterricht required.[1] Forms: https://esk.schulstiftung-ekbo.net/wp-content/uploads/sites/13/2021/12/Anmeldebogen_200325.pdf.[1]

**Fees**: No specific amounts listed on pages; "Elternbeitrag" mentioned but undefined.[1] A PDF form may contain details (likely fee-related: False per scrape, but check for updates).[1]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's mission centers on a **humanist and evangelical educational program** that integrates classical and modern foreign languages, natural sciences, music, art, and sports, fostering comprehensive personal development in the tradition of its 1574 founding.[1][2][3] Core values include independence, freedom, tolerance, respect, responsibility, and Christian ethics, with education viewed as more than knowledge transmission—emphasizing ancient languages for deeper understanding of modern culture, history, and Europe, alongside democracy education through history, politics, religion, and philosophy.[1][3] As an evangelical institution, it promotes Religionsunterricht, student-led Gottesdienste and Andachten, and social engagement like the 10th-grade Sozialpraktikum to encourage ethical action and community involvement.[1][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1574 as the Berlinisches Gymnasium zum Grauen Kloster in a former Franciscan monastery, it is Berlin's oldest Gymnasium; the modern Evangelisches Gymnasium was established in 1949 in Tempelhof, moved to Schmargendorf in 1954, and adopted its current name in 1963, continuing the historical tradition.[1][2][7] Located in Berlin's Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf district (Schmargendorf) at Salzbrunner Str. 41, 14193 Berlin, it operates under the Evangelische Schulstiftung EKBO as a state-recognized private Ersatzschule.[1][2][6] Facilities include new 2023 naturwissenschaftliche Fachräume for physics, biology, and chemistry experiments, supporting digitalization; the community features weekly Andachten, partnerships, and events like Holocaustgedenktag.[1][2]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
A **Gymnasium** offering grundständiger Bildungsgang from 5th grade (Sexta, three classes) and a new one from 7th grade (Quarta, starting 2025/26 with 28 places), leading to Abitur after 12 years; no mentions of MSA or BBR.[1][2][4] Languages: From 5th—English + Latin, 8th—Ancient Greek, 9th—French (elective), 10th—Hebrew (leading to Hebraicum in 12th); Latinum by 10th, Graecum by 11th.[1][2] Other features: Teilungsgruppen in middle school for sciences/math/languages; Profilkurse in 10th; project-based art/music in 8th (e.g., photography, dance, songwriting); subjects span Religion, languages, math-sciences (Math, Physics, Chemistry, Biology/NaWi), social sciences (History, Politics, Geography, Philosophy), and arts-sports (Art, Drama, Music, Dance, Sport).[1]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include AGs (e.g., Debattier AG, Jugend debattiert, Juniorwahlen), Altsprachenfest with ancient texts via music/art/drama, Religionsprojekttag, Orchester/Chöre, Weihnachtskonzert, music/tanzfahrten, theater/tanz/musik projects, Klosterspiele (athletics/team challenges), and top regional Rudern/Hockey.[1] Sports emphasize teams like Fußball/Volleyball for community; arts foster holistic Bildung; social experiences via Sozialpraktikum (10th grade), Silentium, Schülerberatung.[1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Landesweit top in Rudern and Hockey; 2024 Doppeljubiläum (450 years Gymnasium zum Grauen Kloster, 75 years Evangelisches Gymnasium); new Bildungsgang ab 7th grade expands to fourzügig school with diverse Oberstufe offerings; Altsprachenfest 2024 highlighted vibrant classics teaching; no specific awards/rankings or exam outcomes detailed.[1][2][4][7]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions for 2026/27: Applications from Dec 2025; for 5th grade—submit Dec-Jan: grades 1-3 (incl. behavior), birth/baptism certificates, religion/engagement proofs; for 7th—by Feb 13, 2026: grades 1-6, Förderprognose, same certificates; personal interview with leadership/Schulpfarrerin required (starts Dec 4, 2025).[1] Criteria: Good grades across subjects (not sole decider), school-child fit, prior Religionsunterricht preferred; church membership desirable but not required; open to other faiths; submit by post/sekretariat (Tel: 030-8254011, 8:30-15:00); Anmeldeformular available.[1] **No tuition/fee amounts specified** in provided content; mentions Elternbeitrag, Elternhilfswerk, but details likely in PDF: https://eggk.schulstiftung-ekbo.net/wp-content/uploads/form_anmeldung.pdf (not confirmed fee-related).[1]
