description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Freie Schule Anne-Sophie (FSAS) aims to create responsible, open-minded individuals who shape the future through an innovative, bilingual learning environment that fosters mindfulness, tolerance, critical thinking, self-responsibility, and sustainable, equitable action in a changing world.[2][5] Its philosophy emphasizes holistic qualification via individualized coaching, social competence development, self-organized and goal-oriented learning, media literacy, and four core attitudes: appreciation (Wertschätzung), mindfulness (Achtsamkeit), willingness to learn (Lernbereitschaft), and confidence (Zuversicht).[2][3][5] The school positions itself as a state-recognized substitute school in private ownership, prioritizing personality development so "every child leaves as a winner," with a focus on bilingualism, digital responsibility, and social skills in a modern, learning-friendly setting.[1][2][5][7]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 2011 in Berlin with a bilingual primary school and gymnasium; expanded in 2022 to include an entry class (Eingangsklasse).[5] Located at Clayallee 328-334, 14169 Berlin (Steglitz-Zehlendorf district), in a quiet residential area near Monroe-Park, it operates as a full-day school (Ganztagsschule) with modern, aesthetically designed learning spaces, excellent IT equipment, and facilities supporting self-organized learning.[1][2][3][4][9] The community includes "learning partners" (students), coaches/teachers, and emphasizes trust-based relationships, small classes, personal coaching, school festivals, clubs, excursions, and class trips to build community and respect.[1][2][4][5] A second campus exists in Künzelsau, but this profile focuses on Berlin.[6]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Offers bilingual (German-English) full-day education from **Eingangsklasse** (entry class), **Grundschule** (grades 1-6), to **Gymnasium** (grades 7-12), culminating in **Abitur** (recent 2025 graduates noted).[2][4][5][6][10] Half of subjects are taught in German, half in English from grade 1, integrating standard curriculum with self-organized learning, project work, interdisciplinary projects, media competence, digital tools, elective subjects (Wahlpflichtfächer), and personal coaching.[1][2][4][8] State-recognized as a substitute school (Ersatzschule), it emphasizes goal-oriented learning in a structured environment, with full-day operations mandatory from grade 3.[2][5] No mentions of MSA or BBR pathways.[3]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include school festivals, working groups (AGs), clubs, excursions, class trips, and a bound full-day program with after-school care (Hort), promoting community and social skills.[1][2][4][5] Digital focus features responsible, creative media use with top IT infrastructure; bilingual immersion from entry level; personalized coaching and feedback for self-directed learning.[1][2][4][7] Recent events: Halloween ball, school start activities, guest visits (e.g., Staatsminister Gunther Krichbaum).[2] Sports, arts, or exchanges not explicitly detailed, but broad electives and projects support talent development.[3][4]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
State-recognized substitute school; awarded **"Exzellente digitale Schule"** (Excellent Digital School), **"Smart School"**, and **"Apple Distinguished School"** for innovative methods and technology integration.[2][4] Described as "outstanding" private primary and gymnasium with excellent bilingual curriculum; 2025 Abitur graduates celebrated.[1][2][4] Parent review notes rapid language progress (English speaker fluent in German in 6 months).[7] No specific rankings, test scores, or university placement data provided.[1][2]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
Admissions involve info events (next open day: 14.02.2026, weather-dependent), with a "Zur Anmeldung" (to registration) call-to-action and contact form (GDPR-compliant).[2] No detailed requirements listed. Fees are **income-dependent (einkommensabhängiges Schulgeld) starting from 100 Euro** (exact period unspecified); no specifics on registration, materials, meals, or after-school care. No PDFs with fee details found.[2] Scholarships not mentioned.

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's motto is **"Una in via – Gemeinsam auf dem Weg"** (Together on the way), emphasizing a lived Christian worldview that shapes all actions. It views school as an experiential and living space for learning, working, praying, and celebrating, with intensive collaboration among all involved. Education focuses on individual potential, fostering and challenging each student's unique needs within a Christian human image framework.[Main Page][8][9]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded before 1945 (with a 100-year celebration planned for 2026, indicating origins around 1926), the school relocated to Berlin-Westend in 1945 and transitioned to a pure Gymnasium after restructuring. It serves over 700 students (as of 2019/20: 385 girls, 326 boys) with about 75 teachers, owned by the Archdiocese of Berlin. Located in Berlin-Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf (Westend), it includes facilities like a newly designed entrance (90th anniversary), chapel/prayer room, courtyard, new classrooms, course rooms, and cafeteria (added 2010). Accessibility features: parking for disabled, wheelchair-accessible WC, ramp/ground-level access.[2][1][6][About Page]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a state-recognized private **Gymnasium** (grades 5–13, leading to **Abitur**), it follows Berlin's framework curricula with Catholic religious education as a core subject (selectable as advanced course). Languages: English (from grade 5, or earlier in profile classes), Latin (from grade 6, Latinum possible), French, Spanish. **Profile classes** start from grade 5 (20–24 students) for high-achievers, gifted students (including those with comorbidities), featuring reduced exercises, project work, weekly plans, and annual projects from grade 8; integrative approach with 25% spots for gifted/profil children. Upper school (Oberstufe) offers advanced courses in all subjects except performing arts/sports: German, society (history, politics, geography, Catholic religion), math/science (biology, physics, chemistry, informatics), arts (art, music, performing arts). No MSA/BBR mentioned; focuses on Gymnasium/Abitur path.[1][2][3][7][Admissions Page][Curriculum Page][6]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include school ball (Jan 24, 2026), alumni reunion (Jul 3, 2026 for 100-year event), open day (Nov 8, 2025). Emphasis on community: parent council, student representation, support association, prayer/celebration spaces. Profile classes promote social competence, projects, differentiation. No specific sports/arts/exchanges detailed beyond general living space for holistic growth.[Main Page][About Page][Curriculum Page][1]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
No awards, rankings, or specific outcomes (e.g., Abitur results) mentioned in content. School inspection report available (PDF, not fee-related). 100-year milestone in 2026 highlights longevity.[Curriculum Page][2]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Enrollment (7th grade, 2026/27):** Phone appointment Oct 1–Dec 1, 2025 (030-30613013); open day Nov 8, 2025 (9–13). Submit online form or PDF, plus birth certificate copy, original vaccination record (measles required), last 3 report cards (with behavior annex if applicable). No right to admission; decision by principal after interview. Current-year applicants: informal letter + 2 report cards.

**Profile class (5th grade):** Application by Dec 11, 2025 (pre-Winter break), with parental letter, form, grades (1–3 full, 4th half-year + prognosis by Zeugnistag), photo, child description. ~40 invited to Jan familiarization days/interviews; decisions in Winter break.[5][Admissions Page]

**Fees:** Income-based from Aug 1, 2024: 3.4% of taxable income (25,000–150,000 EUR) + progression reserve income (§32b EStG); minimum 20 EUR/child/month below 25,000 EUR. Sibling discount for Archdiocese schools. No fixed amounts, registration/material/meal/aftercare/scholarship details provided.[Admissions Page]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Katholische Theresienschule is a free Roman Catholic gymnasium sponsored by the Archdiocese of Berlin, emphasizing a Catholic educational profile with mandatory religious education (Catholic Religion) as a core subject, alongside optional evangelical religious instruction.[1][3][4] It integrates faith-based values into school life through events like Schulgottesdienst (school church services), Schulpastoralaktion “Post mit Herz” (pastoral action sending cards to seniors), and Advent activities such as concerts and markets, promoting community service and Christian charity.[1][7] The school's approach fosters holistic development, blending academic rigor with practical, ethical, and social learning in a coeducational, inclusive environment open to Catholic, Protestant, and non-denominational students.[1][3]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1894 as a girls' gymnasium initiated by fathers of the Herz-Jesu-Kirche in Berlin-Prenzlauer Berg, the school moved to its current location at Behaimstr. 29, 13086 Berlin (Weißensee, Pankow district) in 1991 due to growing enrollment, now serving approximately 700 students.[1][2] It transitioned to coeducation in 1991/1992 and established a full gymnasium track starting in grade 5 from 1998/1999; a new sports hall was inaugurated in 2012.[1] Facilities include a school-owned cafeteria managed by "3 Köche" since 2010/2011 with adjusted break times for dining, accessibility features (parking for disabled, wheelchair elevator, accessible WC), and a virtual tour option.[1][3][4] Community ties are strong with the adjacent St. Josef Church and Erzbistum Berlin sponsorship.[1][7]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a state-recognized private Gymnasium (school number 03P10), it offers grades 5-12 (gymnasialer Grundstand from grade 5, leading to Abitur after G8 or G9), with open all-day care (OGB), language emphasis, and classical tracks (Latin from grade 5).[2][3][4] Core languages include English (S1), French/Spanish (3rd foreign language), Latin; upper-level performance courses in English/French; Italian via AG and Model United Nations; participation in "The Big Challenge" foreign language competition.[3][4] Subjects follow a broad curriculum including Religion, with additional offerings in psychology, history, literature, and politics; pathways lead to Abitur (evidenced by strong 2020 average grade of 1.7, above Berlin average).[3][4][5] Practical elements include internships (e.g., grade 9 Betriebspraktikum) and science excursions like radioactivity labs.[7]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars feature AGs (working groups) such as Malteser (first aid, fundraising via Kuchenbasar for uninsured medical care), Zumba, Klima AG (climate, Adventbasar), Darstellendes Spiel (performing arts, theater workshops at Deutsches Theater/Jungen DT), Schulsanitäter, and more including KSJ (youth groups), mediation, school newspaper, and choir (Adventskonzert).[3][7] Sports include open Zumba training and a dedicated sports hall; trips encompass SV-Fahrt (youth trips to Wünsdorf), class excursions, exchanges, wandertage (hiking days), and cultural events like Cinéfête, British Christmas Market.[1][5][7] Social initiatives promote health, service (e.g., 150€ donations fund medical exams), and pastoral actions.[7]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Student Felix Wiede (10b) won the school-level Diercke-Wissen Geography Competition 2026, advancing to state level after prior grade 8 victory.[7] Abitur 2020 results: average grade 1.7 (above Berlin state average), with 5 students at 1.0 and 3 at 1.1.[5] Participation in competitions like Geschichtswettbewerb des Bundespräsidenten, political education contests, Theo literature prize, and Vorlesewettbewerb.[3] No broader rankings or certifications noted in sources.

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Enrollment Process**: Targets students with Gymnasialempfehlung entering grades 5 or 7; for 2026/2027, applications closed. Process includes online/postal application (formulars provided), mandatory grade 5 entrance test (10 Jan 2026, 10:00-12:00), interviews from Nov 2025 (grade 7)/Dec 2025 (grade 5), open day (11 Oct 2025), info evening (14 Oct 2025). Required documents: photo, birth/baptism certificates, vaccination record (measles), grade reports (3rd/5th grade, half-year 4th/6th), behavior info; un-baptized need consent for Catholic RE. Deadlines: grade 7 (4 Nov 2025), grade 5 (24 Nov 2025); additional docs due 30 Jan 2026.[7]

**Fees**: No exact tuition amounts listed on pages; regulated by Erzbistum Berlin (links to Schulgeldregelungen PDF, Antrag auf Schulgeldermäßigung, Aussetzung Schulgeld). Forms include applications for reductions/exemptions (e.g., second child), contract sample (under revision). Meals via external provider (www.drei-koeche.de, no costs specified). No registration, material, after-school, or scholarship amounts detailed; income-based reductions available via forms.[7]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The Moser-Schule Schweizer Gymnasium emphasizes **academic, digital, and social competencies** through action-oriented, student-centered teaching, comprehensive study and career orientation, and a dynamic learning environment tailored to students' needs.[Main Page] Core values include responsibility, loyalty, respect, adaptability, and reliability, instilled from the start.[Main Page] It promotes immersive multilingual education in German, French, and English to develop responsible "digital citizens" with modern digital tools and social-pedagogical support.[Main Page][About Page]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded in 1961 in Geneva by Henri Moser as a French private school for foreign parents' children, the innovative family-owned enterprise expanded to Nyon and Berlin in 2006 under Alain Moser (General Director since 2001) and Pia Effront.[Main Page][3] The Berlin campus, a state-recognized private Gymnasium in Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf (Badenallee 31-32, 14052 Berlin), serves grades 5-12 with small classes of 20 students (two parallel classes per year starting in grade 5).[Main Page][Fees/Tuition Page] Facilities include modern digital equipment, block scheduling (90-minute periods), and support from social pedagogy and school psychology; it offers full-day care with homework supervision.[Main Page][About Page]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
Serves **grades 5-12** (Sekundarstufe I: 5-10; Sekundarstufe II: 11-12) as a state-recognized Gymnasium with German-French-English focus.[Main Page] Immersion teaching from grade 5: 50% subjects in German, 50% in French/English (e.g., grades 5-6: Art, Music, Geography, half of Science in French; English as additional language; no prior French required).[About Page] Grade 10: Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA) for upper secondary entry; grade 12: **Abitur** or **AbiBac** (French Baccalauréat dual diploma, requiring prior French subject proof).[Main Page][Fees/Tuition Page] Block lessons start at 8:30 AM, end ~15:15 (four days/week); language certifications include mandatory DELF (grade 7) and optional Cambridge (grades 5-12).[About Page] Leistungskurse in Biology, German, English, French, Math; third languages Latin/Spanish.[4]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
**Extracurriculars** include homework supervision (90 minutes daily post-lessons), working groups (AGs) like theater, sport climbing, tennis, hip-hop, salsa, chess, "School without Racism – School with Courage"; class trips, exchange programs with partner schools in Geneva, Nyon, and Paris; internships, interdisciplinary projects, study trips, and course exchanges in upper grades.[Main Page][About Page] Sports and arts integrate into AGs and immersion (e.g., bilingual Art/Music).[About Page][1] Full-day offers reduce homework load via block scheduling.[About Page]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
Holds **Qualitätssiegel Berlin für exzellente berufliche Orientierung** (2014-2018) and **Qualitätssiegel für exzellente digitale Schulen** (2019-2023).[1] Prepares for Abitur/AbiBac, DELF/Cambridge certifications, with events like Career Day, Abitur results announcements, and Zukunftstag.[Main Page] No specific exam results, rankings, or recent awards detailed beyond seals.[1][4]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Enrollment process**: Open year-round interviews with parents/child and school leadership (no fixed periods; early booking advised as grade 5 spots—two classes of 20—fill from prior year).[Fees/Tuition Page] Grade 5-6: Post-grades 1-4, gymnasial recommendation advantageous, suitability required, immersion focus.[Fees/Tuition Page] Grades 7-10: If prior knowledge/places available.[Fees/Tuition Page] Grade 11: Post-MSA; AbiBac needs prior French proof.[Fees/Tuition Page] Enrollment for full school year.

**Fees** (monthly in 12 rates, March 2024; payable annually or monthly; subject to review; sibling discounts: 20% second child, 40% third+; income-based options available on request pre-contract for low income):  
- Grades 5-6: **€400/month**  
- Grades 7-10: **€430/month**  
- Grades 11-12: **€445/month**  
**Registration fee** (one-time, new students): **€500** (reduced to **€100** if family gross annual income ≤ €29,420, credited to first half-year tuition).  
**Additional costs**: Meals, after-school care, school materials, admin fees (amounts unspecified).[Fees/Tuition Page] No material/meal/after-school specifics; no PDFs found.[Fees/Tuition Page]

description:
### 1. MISSION AND EDUCATIONAL PHILOSOPHY
The school's mission emphasizes strengthening motivation and social competencies, fostering close connections between school, family, and society, and preparing students for professional challenges and societal engagement through individualized support and global readiness.[1][2] Core values include critical thinking via active discussion and practical experiences, respect, safety, well-being, and participatory decision-making, with a holistic education combining academic excellence, creativity, and personal development in small classes (under 15 students, 1:15 teacher-student ratio).[1][2][4] The educational approach prioritizes individualized learning plans, innovative methods, bilingual immersion (primarily English), international orientation, and modern didactics like immersion techniques with native speakers to enhance cognitive flexibility, intercultural competence, and future skills.[1][4][5]

### 2. HISTORY, COMMUNITY AND FACILITIES
Founded over 15 years ago, the Privates Europa-Gymnasium Berlin is a state-recognized private substitute school (Ersatzschule in freier Trägerschaft, Sekundarstufe I) operated by Privates Europa-Gymnasium Berlin gGmbH, located in Berlin's Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf district at Haubachstr. 5 (or 8), 10585 Berlin.[1][2][9] The community features a diverse student body from over 30 nations, with close parent-teacher collaboration, a focus on community through projects and events, and a safe learning environment with digital and physical security measures.[1][4][5] Facilities include modern digital equipment (hardware/software with annual service fee), specialized rooms, and spaces supporting small groups, though specific details like size or labs are not detailed.[1][5]

### 3. CURRICULUM AND ACADEMIC PROGRAMS
As a Gymnasium (grades 7-12), it offers the Mittlerer Schulabschluss (MSA) and Abitur after 8 years (G8), with intensive exam preparation, performance courses in English and social sciences, and profiles in English, mathematics, economics/social sciences.[2][3][7][9] Bilingual instruction uses immersion in English (for subjects like geography, history, biology) alongside German, with additional languages including Spanish, French, Mandarin (courses), and Chinese cultural elements; native speakers teach for authenticity.[1][4][5][7][9] Programs emphasize individualized paths, STEM focus (math, informatics, natural sciences, technology), key competencies, and holistic development with structured schedules starting at 9:00, all-day care, and 100% success rate claimed.[1][2][3]

### 4. STUDENT LEARNING EXPERIENCES
Extracurriculars include diverse working groups (AGs) in arts, music, sports; cultural workshops, Mandarin courses, Tai Chi and traditional Chinese sports; international exchanges and partnerships; excursions, class trips; and projects promoting talents and societal participation.[1][4][5] Daily experiences feature interactive, practical learning in small groups, creativity encouragement, and global perspectives, with full-day supervision (1:1 care claimed) and events building community.[1][2]

### 5. SCHOOL ACHIEVEMENTS AND OUTCOMES
The school claims a **100% success rate** in qualifications, with "best Abschlüsse" for the future, supported by small classes and preparation courses, though no specific awards, rankings, or external certifications are mentioned.[1][2][3] Outcomes focus on academic excellence, bilingual proficiency, and personal growth for university/career paths.[3][5]

### 6. ADMISSIONS AND FEES
**Enrollment process**: Three steps—an inquiry for a consultation (phone/email/form), personal advising assessing personality, motivation, social behavior beyond grades/recommendations (including trial day), and final admission welcoming diversity and potential; open to regular starts or transfers (e.g., grade 7).[1][Admissions Page] Contact: info@europa-gymnasium.eu, +49 30 740 737 17.[Admissions Page][9]

**Fees**: No exact EUR amounts listed on pages (no PDFs found with fee details). Tuition is **monthly, individually determined** based on family income, household size, marital status, care obligations, and hardships—fair, transparent, not a barrier to admission. Discounts: sibling (2nd child 25%, 3rd 50%, 4+ 75%); loyalty (10% after 6 years, 20% after 10; from year 4 for grade 7 transfers). Additional: one-time administration fee (upon contract), annual service pauschale for tech, costs for materials, uniforms, meals, trips. Support: reductions/stipends for low-income, high performers with good behavior, or Bürgergeld recipients; raten options.[Fees Page]
